This is really odd. Most stable Originium in the world would not metastasize or grow. But the stable Originium near the Originium Gorge exhibits signs of slow and steady growth. 

I don't know what's affecting it. 

We've established a surveillance camp and triangulated the source via our nearby mining sites. All readings point to something within the Originium Gorge. But the Originium formations inside the place is just too thick. There's no way we can head in and gather more details or visual readings. 

Perhaps we can use our prospecting machines. Upgrading them might give us a solution.

114528DB55036C72:
Investigations on the Illegal Endfield Industries Aircraft Moving into Final Phase 
Published February 9, Mossfield 
(Correspondent: Irina) 

Ongoing investigation on the Endfield Industries aircraft that crashed 3 months ago just released their latest findings. 

Professional investigators concluded that the primary causes of the crash of the Endfield aircraft 3 months ago are illegal modifications and exceeding the designed load limit. In response to further inquiries by our correspondent, investigators further expressed that the aircraft was not registered at the proper administrative institution. Crash site and wreckage analyses further revealed that Endfield demonstrated inadequacies in aircraft modification techniques and created an aircraft with critical safety risks. As for exceeding the load limit, investigators confided that they were unable to deduce what the Endfield aircraft was carrying, but large, heavy weapon platforms may be a possibility.

16CA489CE2AB1788:
Having traveled the wildlands and fighting non-human lifeforms on her own for many years, Operator Ember exhibits a rather strange mental state. It is hard to imagine the events that forced her to adopt such a grueling and painful way of life. 

Ember's perception of time is also quite unlike the others. If kept indoors with no direct access to the natural environment or open skies, she would slip into a state of obliviousness and become wholly unaware of the passing of time unless reminded by others. 

Nevertheless, most do not agree that Ember has "time blindness". Many Operators have ratified that Ember is capable of using terms such as "5 minutes later" or "9 o'clock position" in her tactical commands. Hence, the Medical Division has ruled out dyschronometria and concludes that Ember would only subconsciously enter a state of extended focus in various conditions. Under this state, the passing of time holds little meaning for Ember. 

"Xaihi, how many days will it take for us to walk to the nearest Endfield observation post?" 
"Hmm ... 3 days? Perhaps I should say ... the time it takes for you to terminate 600 minor Aggeloi." 
"Understood. Gratitude, Xaihi." 
"You're welcome."

29584D34D678806B:
Hey! Bruh! Worried about going broke, going nomadic, and dying out there in the wildlands? 
Hey! Gurl! Scared about getting fired with no backup and forced to starve as a street hobo? 
It's nothing to be scared about! And stop worrying already! 'Tis time to get practical! 
Finish this book and you'll become a master wildlands survivalist like me! 

Food 
Every beast is a source of food, but I don't recommend going after the docile herbivores. Not that they're dangerous, but these natural lawnmowers do nothing but eat grass, drink water, and then run away from predators when they show up. Their lives depend on being able to run like hell and that's how nature made them, friends! Don't ever bet your weakness against their strength. But if you're a Kuranta, you can give it a shot because you're built for running. Now, we can wield the Originium Arts. What about animals? They can't. That's for sure. So if you're looking for some game meat, just wait for a predator to show up, blast them with your Arts, and then get a barbecue going. I recommend pan frying meat with feathermoss because it's really good. 

Alright. Now get yourself out there in the wildlands and start learning how to survive!

Test results reveal that Ember is extremely skilled in squad-level combat and demonstrates exceptional physicals and combat techniques. The HR division has conducted a simple interview with Ember and we are confident that her experience in the Order of Steel Oath was instrumental in shaping her excellent military skills. 

In actual combat, Ember is skilled in optimizing the performance of her heavy loadout to achieve remarkable resilience and unleash incredible destruction. She often uses her equipment to suppress large groups of enemies and dominate the battlefield. Ember is also an outstanding team player in squad-level battles, and is capable of devising and executing effective battle plans on the fly. 

Though we are unsure how the Order of Steel Oath rated Ember's military talents, she has proved to be a first-rate Endfield Operator.

LOG DATE: <@nar.mark>1234</> <@nar.mark>December</> <@nar.mark>12</>. 
ISSUE (ONE LINE ONLY): Something's wrong with the tracks. 
REPAIR DATE: <@nar.mark>1212</> <@nar.mark>December</> <@nar.mark>12</>. 
REPAIR NOTES: Nothing's wrong with the track. It's the link between the track module and the master control module. Sure, you can pile the crates on the forklift's lifting forks, but DO NOT pile them on the seat or in the cabin. Those Endfielders sell us really sensitive stuff that can't take a hit. 

LOG DATE: <@nar.mark>1212</> <@nar.mark>December</> <@nar.mark>12</>
ISSUE (ONE LINE ONLY): It won't move no more. 
REPAIR DATE: <@nar.mark>1212</> <@nar.mark>December</> <@nar.mark>12</>. 
REPAIR NOTES: The smart control module will shut down if it overheats. When you get a fire going next time, keep your distance. The Endfielders never thought that workers like us might need to start a warming fire next to the machinery. I know it makes no sense. Why would anyone place the smart control module RIGHT NEXT to the power supply module? Don't they even think about heat dissipation? Maybe they're trying to stuff everything into that tiny chassis.

33B8E48EBF14F945:
Operator Xaihi is a professional Originium Circuitry technologist with vast and unique knowledge on Originium communications technology as well as software and hardware engineering. Many complicated issues were only resolved using Xaihi's unique and often astounding approaches. Interestingly, when the engineers asked Xaihi to guide them along her thought process that led to the solution, they would only receive this answer: "It's just the way it is." This led to an inside joke within Endfield that "Xaihi opened her eyes and sees the world only as strings of codes and numbers." 

Operator Xaihi's personality is calm and gentle, and acting with knowledge that far exceeds her age and often conversing with a technical vocabulary that goes beyond layman's understanding. She is curious about everything and always eager to experience different lifestyles that also include that of a frontline soldier. The Endfield Engineering Center (EFEC) crafted a custom Arts Unit for her just to satisfy her request of experiencing true combat in person. Xaihi personally designed this Arts Unit core to resonate with her special Art, creating crystalline shards to attack her opponents from afar.

33BEA631D436C983:
Too many nosy people around. So I wrote it down on paper. Burn it when you're done reading. 
The Landbreakers took our camp and all of our supplies and cash. I sneaked over there and saw what they did. The Elbee scum literally took everything AND mama's kitchen sink. 
I got a plan to deal with these greedy scab eaters. 
We'll come up with a few obvious and subtle hints, graffiti, and letters that hint at possible secret hoards. Some of the Elbees are bound to bite the bait and do their own digging in secret. When that happens, we'll show them what we're made of. 
No one is the eternal prey. And no one is the eternal hunter.

4128F7FC1C14C3EB:
HOWEVER, the power of innovation lost to the Flaming Reins did not just disappear! In fact, I think the Shield of Nearl is the one that picked up the tactical essentials once practiced by the Flaming Reins and added their own innovations to it. 

Let's first analyze the tech of their loadouts. Based on the equipment stats published by the Shield of Nearl, they will be using Raythean Striker series support kits. The kits are pretty new to the market and the numbers still need to be verified out in the field—only the most adventurous teams are willing to try them out. 

Now let's take a closer look at the 3v3 solo tactics used by the Shield of Nearl. After the new Codes were introduced, 3v3 solos for MMAB Majors became attrition slugfests and offered a great deal less season points. In the previous issue, we analyzed the fact that the new system was put in place to better identify MVPs and how Hongshan teams came up with the Kuranta Strategy to deal with these new rules. So we won't go further into this. So, in response to the structure of the games, various clubs would tend to avoid deploying their best fighters first to avoid unnecessary attrition. But the Shield of Nearl chose to start the bout with Crimson Shield with extremely powerful Originium Arts that unfortunately drains the warrior quickly. Of course, this is an improvement over the "Assassin" tactic that the Flaming Reins first came up with last year. So the play is to have Crimson Shield start the attack, take down 1 opponent and whittle down the second, and follow that up by deploying Armored Shield known for defensive counters. The core tactics of this phase is an attritional grind to finish off the second opponent. The Shield of Nearl captain also has excellent ratings in various aspects of combat... 

(The rest has been torn off)

42854518267D7385: Alright ... power is coming back. The engineering team will be in charge of re-activating our auto-defense systems. Things are finally getting better.
428D6BD4A381933D: Hey! Bruh! Worried about going broke, going nomadic, and dying out there in the wildlands? Hey! Gurl! Scared about getting fired with no backup and forced to starve as a street hobo? It's nothing to be scared about! And stop worrying already! 'Tis time to get practical! Finish this book and you'll become a master wildlands survivalist like me! WATER The most important thing about surviving in the wildlands is to locate a source of clean, potable water. Such essential resources are usually controlled by the most powerful local gang or settlement, so we best avoid those places because we don't want to have those ultra-violent freaks pointing guns at our heads. And the same goes for the nomad settlements. Trust me, pal. Nomads who scrape wildland dirt for a living have hair-triggers that may be far more dangerous than bandit weapons! And you're definitely no master wildlands survivalist if you go begging for water from those hobo nomads! So, we must find plants. The feathermoss common in the wilderness are tough tiny plants that love water and tend to grow near aquifers. They also store plenty of fluids in their tissues. So if you can't locate a source of water near a patch of feathermoss, grab a few and chew on those to stave off the thirst! FOOD Every beast is a source of food, but I don't recommend going after the docile herbivores. Not that they're dangerous, but these natural lawnmowers do nothing but eat grass, drink water, and then run away from predators when they show up. Their lives depend on being able to run like hell and that's how nature made them, friends! Don't ever bet your weakness against their strength. But if you're a Kuranta, you can give it a shot because you're built for running. Now, we can wield the Originium Arts. What about animals? They can't. That's for sure. So if you're looking for some game meat, just wait for a predator to show up, blast them with your Arts, and then get a barbecue going. I recommend pan frying meat with feathermoss because it's really good. Alright. Now get yourself out there in the wildlands and start learning how to survive!
42C271F6EF2A36F5:
Partial Logistics Deadlock in the Mossfield Region, UWST Administration Crisis Feared 
Published February 9, Mossfield 
(Correspondent: Irina) 

Valley IV of the Mossfield region finds itself deep in the mire of a logistics deadlock. This may be yet another crisis that exposes severe issues within the UWST. 

The experimental base of Valley IV has always been a focal point for many interested parties. But the UWST-designed logistics established to support this site has been subject of criticism shortly after its completion. 

Certain experts expressed that building transport infrastructure is difficult in the area. In fact, a standard approach to logistics and transport infrastructure should have been avoided in the first place. 

Of course, the relatively isolated nature of UWST administration also meant that they are only capable of confused and chaotic response to various contingencies.

After joining Endfield, Wulfgard became a topic of discussion amongst many Operators for quite a while. 

Many could not understand why Supervisor Perlica would invite this dangerous man into Endfield Industries, especially since he had infiltrated the HQ and even attempted to abduct her before. Perlica did little to address such doubts, but gave a simple line that clearly stated her attitude: "I appreciate Wulfgard's abilities, and have faith that he will be an Operator well worth our long-term investments." 

Many fellow Endfielders do not really know Wulfgard as the Operator actively avoids social situations. 

Many, except for Supervisor Perlica and select members of her Crisis Team, continue to respond with these lines when they hear or see the name "Wulfgard": 
"Who's this? A new Operator?" 
"Since when did we hire this guy?" 
"Oh. I think I saw this name in the Weapons Maintenance Budget Approval Report ... but I've never seen him in person." 

The mystery figure doesn't seem to mind. In fact, he's quite happy about this. 
"This is good," the young man commented as he packed the combat supplies, his tone betraying no sense of emotions whatsoever, "Because in most circumstances, being anonymous is far safer than being a celebrity..." 

Wulfgard proved to be surprisingly cooperative in team operations and easy to get along with, so long as you maintain proper "social distancing" with him.

Recently, we have received reports of anomalous changes to work environments in the lower mine shafts in the Valley Pass. 

Investigations revealed that reports of "ferocious roars from the underground" came from fellow workers who are under-rested. The deep sounds of machinery mixed with movement of loose ore were mistakenly interpreted as the "ferocious roars". 

However, we are aware that the Valley Pass experienced a Blight rupture event over 10 years ago. We cannot rule out the possibility that certain elements from that disaster may have persisted till this day.

66F1A0DC7B5B8F26:
When the original Angelina's application for the Heterocore Project was approved after a thorough and meticulous review process, she left a letter for her future self. After reading it, the heterocore of Angelina was filled with hopes for her own journey across Talos-II. 

The Angelina heterocore had to undergo multiple stages of adaptive and stability testing to ensure that her mental and physical parameters are within acceptable limits. Only then was she allowed to perform certain activities. After completing memory tests reviewed by both Endfield and Rhodes Island, Angelina was finally allowed to start working as a Catastrophe Messenger on Talos-II. As predicted by her test results, Angelina quickly adapted and welcomed life on Talos-II, and was soon dispatched to Endfield Industries. 

Angelina is curious about her other self as well as Talos-II. She knew about the recorded past of the original Angelina and believes that she was an excellent and dependable friend to those around her. She would often try to get information about the other Angelina, and see if her findings would overlap with aspects from memory fragments that are similar but not entirely the same as herself. 

Angelina wants to excel just as her senpai did, and this made her look forward to creating her own story and future. She wishes to have her own encounters upon this uncharted world.

83AC908EEA861AD5:
Largest Landbreaker Warband of the Desert Annihilated 
Published February 9, Desert 
(Correspondent: Carleigh) 

The largest Landbreaker warband of the Desert region has been formally annihilated thanks to a multi-faction effort. 

Investigations by our correspondent has confirmed that Giga-Maw, the largest Landbreaker warband of the Desert region, is no more. Their chief, "Flat Arc" Anderson, was also killed in the battle. 

The operation to destroy the Giga-Maws lasted several months. After successfully inserting undercover elites into the warband, a total of over one thousand combatants from various factions were deployed. The enemy was taken by surprise and utterly crushed in a single battle. 

Despite the overwhelming success of the joint anti-banditry operation, our correspondent would still remind every resident of the Desert region to remain vigilant of roaming bandits and criminals on the run from justice.

85E4FFB13476E440:
Too many nosy people around. So I wrote it down on paper. Burn it when you're done reading. 
The Landbreakers took our camp and all of our supplies and cash. I sneaked over there and saw what they did. The Elbee scum literally took everything AND mama's kitchen sink. 

I got a plan to deal with these greedy scab eaters.
We'll come up with a few obvious and subtle hints, graffiti, and letters that hint at possible secret hoards. Some of the Elbees are bound to bite the bait and do their own digging in secret. When that happens, we'll show them what we're made of.

Out of respect for personal privacy, we do not recommend asking too much about Operator Xaihi's past. But when Xaihi helped resolve the system-level incident in the lab, many senior EFEC staff became extremely curious about her background and how she managed to acquire such immense knowledge. 

"I asked her the question. She only told me that she 'treads upon the path' without further explanation..." said a clueless Operator who took part in a field operation with Xaihi. 

Xaihi and Azrila the Ember both reported for duty at Endfield Industries on the same day. When the two first met, Xaihi had already spent an year traveling around on her own. Given her innate curiosity about everything, Xaihi quickly became extremely interested in Ember and why the knight would tirelessly fight the Aggeloi alone. Spurred on by such questions and thanks to the knight's tacit understanding, the two traveled together for a while. 

REMINDER: Despite Operator Xaihi's immense knowledge, she exhibits significant deficiencies in basic understanding of daily living and activities (common sense) and should be kept away from solo activities.

9444D8198D82DCC5:
When Perlica just took up the role of Supervisor, many Endfielders were doubtful if this young girl could properly lead Endfield Industries—despite the fact that she was personally taken in and mentored by the Endministrator. Nevertheless, Perlica quickly proved herself with actual results and achievements. 

Perlica gave the research division additional support and propelled Endfield into a new stage of Techno-Protocol mastery. Many research findings finally found practical use and orders from across the world expanded the company's range of services, further dispelling doubts on Perlica's leadership. 

Despite being a manager of sorts, Perlica would spend most of the time in the front line. At the end of every mission, she would also give several days of leave for the Operators in her team. 

"Burning yourself out will do you no favor. Go and enjoy a vacation."

We are an industrial and construction company. Endfield is actively engaged in organizing and promoting construction projects that expand our frontiers. And by expanding our frontiers, we specifically mean terraforming and large-scale building on inhospitable land. 

As an industrial and construction company, we are now doing our best to promote our Automated Industry Complex system. The AIC is a comprehensive factory solution that primarily features automation and modular facilities. 

Endfield believes that the AIC is the definitive answer to help humanity survive the perilous world of Talos-II.

959D770ED32D66B8:
Chen Qianyu is a scion of the renowned ancient House of Chen and never shies away from talking about about her illustrious family. When it comes to personal dreams and ambitions, Chen would often mention Ch'en Hui-chieh, one of the most illustrious figures of her clan. 

Chen grew up listening to the glorious tales of this family legend and treated the heroine as her role model. She would dedicate herself to mastering the family sword art while generously helping anyone in need. 

But Chen hardly talks about her own parents, and when she does, it would be something about her given name—Qianyu, a name that contains a reminder from a mother. 

Of course, Chen would be more interested in topics on self-improvement. When off-duty, Chen is most likely to be found in the training gym. Several Operators have reported seeing Chen walking about of the gym at 2 to 3 am in the morning. Chen often ends her 8-hour intensive training sessions with just a few minor bruises and hardly any sign of fatigue. Witnessing such strength and dedication, fellow Crisis Team member Wulfgard even exclaimed: "I only read about such characters in fiction novels." 

Although Operator Chen demonstrated exemplary performance in the Endfield Operator Proficiency Assessment, she always rated her own combat skills with comments like "never enough". No one is certain about the goal that she wants to reach, but joining Endfield just might very well be a part of her long journey.

From his pathological reports, Gore has always been mentally healthy. His childhood background shows that he was smart and likable, and had many friends. In fact, he was a genius. Most importantly, he was a humble genius and a rare gem with great potential. His school teachers, principal, family members, and friends always expected Gore to do something important and most of all, ingenious. They thought he would get into emerging technology research, find employment at a large corporation, and become a corporate officer with the TGCC. That's the typical path laid out for geniuses like him. It was supposed to be his future, and he was expected to complete the journey nicely. 

Indeed, that was what everyone expected. However, after he graduated from college and started his first corporate internship, things began changing a little.

According to test results, Chen has outstanding physicals and reflexes. The suite of combat techniques she uses are based on various types of traditional martial arts. During combat, Chen can quickly suppress her opponent with a rapid flurry of strikes and seize the initiative. Few field Operators of Endfield could hope to stand against her attacks. When not in battle, Chen's warrior instincts often gave timely warnings during critical moments and helped her party avoid certain dangers. 

Operator Chen is also determined to hone her combat techniques and is more than happy to spar with others. However, given her unique background and training, Chen would hardly get anything useful from others. Most of her advices are rather impractical as well.

A94826836111D412:
According to test results, this Operator received the "EXCELLENT" rating in both physical stamina and strength. She also demonstrates outstanding battlefield mobility. 

Avywenna is a self-proclaimed polearm expert and her combat techniques feature both power and speed. 

Even before she became an Endfielder, Avywenna already has quite a reputation as an Armed Messenger in the frontier. Her career statistics show a near 100% delivery no matter how dangerous the route nor how desolated the destination may be, and has accumulated beaming reviews in escorting important cargo or VIPs. 

A review of past contracts with corporate partners showed that Operator Avywenna leveraged her status as a top Messenger to demand rather expensive rates. Nevertheless, she agreed to a much more reasonable compensation package when joining Endfield Industries. 

We do not know her reasons for doing so, but this should not form the basis to doubt her motives or abilities.

B426BFB57CA89C75:
(This is a page torn out from a sports magazine.) 

Everyone knows that the Shield of Nearl is the rising star of this Mixed Martial Arts of the Band (MMAB) season. Three dirt poor lads hailing from the wildlands managed to cut a bloody path to the finals. Such audacity is a literal slap on the face of what we now regard as modern competitive combat arts. That's right! This is my direct jab at the defending champion of this season—the Kazimierz Flaming Reins CC, and my expression of fair criticism at their combat gear and tactics. 

And I'm definitely still on topic. Will the Shield of Nearl CC seize the champion's trophy? That will depend on the Kazimierz Flaming Reins CC, because I don't know if they're waking up from their dazed stupor. 

Up till the eve of the grand finals, most of the audience, me included, have utmost confidence in the Flaming Reins. Why? Because our team is the only two-time champion ever since MMAB Majors updated the Codes of the Game. They're not messing around! It is an unprecedented achievement! 

But will the Flaming Reins continue to check their challengers? 
Well, I don't think so! Because the Flaming Reins are now a bunch of hibernating beasts that fight with the most unimaginative ways.

BD60A41EB471BF96:
What is the world of jianghu? The society of rivers and lakes? 

Let's just say if you really want to ask this question and managed to find someone to ask. If the person being asked is a young pugilist who just finished his training at a Hongshan martial arts academy, he would say the jianghu is a forest of chilling blades awash with crimson wine. Don't take this literally, though. 

But is jianghu really that simple? Of course not. You can press me for an answer, and I won't be able to give you one. 

Your temper might be flaring when you reached this point and think that I'm just fooling around with you. But trust me, I'm not. I'm not a jianghu vagabond and I have yet to fully explore this special underworld. A forced answer will be most inappropriate. I can only give you a story. A story about a hero. 

And I'll place my precious fingers in this bet. Three of them, in fact. Listen close—the jianghu is right in his eyes.

BFDB1A9C51E8992C:
That night, Gore drove out of the city he calls home. Of course, he only went to a nearby wilderness where he knew a gang of people. This gang of bandits would appear like law-abiding civilians when they have money but engage in murder and raiding when they don't. Rumors also mentioned that these criminals did some secret, dirty work for the TGCC and that was why nobody ever found evidence of their vile acts. Gore drove straight into their camp and I suspect that he actually did some work to locate them since this bandit hideout was not easy to find. Anyway, Gore went right to the lair, opened the trunk of his car, fished out a prototype weapon freshly put together in his lab, and then unleashed hell on the shoddy sentry post. 

So, you see... Oh, before I forget. Gore's lab focuses on the miniaturization of city-defense weapons.

E4B56EB4A416BA1E: It was the hottest day of the entire summer. In fact, it was so hot that it would cook any demon out in the open. When the sun began to set on cloud days like this, it would smother everything on land with a thick layer of yellow. A most frustrating shade of yellow. Gore's neighbor said that she saw Gore coming back from work as he normally would. He even said hello when he went downstairs to toss the trash. This neighbor, a kindly lady, gave Gore a few caring words and told him to get some rest, and the young man responded with a polite smile. This lady then remembered that she felt the "young man looked a little dazed and needed to catch up on sleep."

E8A0A38FB97B1518:
The Terrans have tried using Originium energy since ancient times. A variety of ways were used to activate this mineral and release the energy contained within and to channel and convert the form of said energy. Various techniques powered using Originium energy are known as the Originium Arts. From the most primitive way of burning Originium for heat to utilizing the mineral and its derivatives in industrial systems, humanity has never ceased its study and use of this crystalline material. 

However, the very use of Originium is also accompanied by significant risks. 

Activated Originium will gradually disintegrate into tiny Originium particles that can infect inadequately protected individuals. After entering the body, these particles will slowly crystallize within the victim and become new Originium formations. Such condition is described as Oripathy. The Originium in the bodies of those infected with Oripathy may also continue to infect others. 

The people of Talos-II have inherited the results of Originium research from the Age of Terra and even made further technological progress. The Talosians now enjoy cleaner Originium industries and technologies, and have made remarkable achievements in Oripathy treatment.

E9AEB7E6620EDEC9:
And thus Gore started his jianghu career with the first pull of the trigger. This heroic legend then went after the bandits and blasted them to bits using all kinds of heavy weapons. I met him once in the wilderness. The man sat on a stone polishing his weapon, and he smiled at me. 

I asked him why he wanted to zero those bandits. He told me he just wanted to spend as little money as possible to unleash the carnage and all those filthy ideas festering within his mind. 

I then asked him how he would head back to the city after that murderous spree. 

Gore smiled. He told me that he did not kill a single human. 
I thought about it and his words made a great deal of sense to me. Whatever he shot up can hardly be called human, after all.

F0C33D665DFF6AFC:
Our corporate organization is divided into 4 major modules: 

<@qu.key>Facility Technology and Production Module</> 
The Facility Technology and Production Module (FACTEP) is the leading production division of Endfield Industries. FACTEP manages many Endfield production sites as well as corporate technology research. The module is further subdivided into other divisions that include the Engineering Division (EF-ED) and multiple factories. 

<@qu.key>Services and Maintenance Module</> 
The Services and Maintenance Module (SERVMAIN) is responsible for internal operations and maintenance of Endfield Industries. 

<@qu.key>Defense Module</> 
The Defense Module (DEF) is mainly responsible for various security and protection services in Endfield Industries. It is the channel by which Endfield exercises our legal rights for armed solutions. 

<@qu.key>Business and Partnership Services</> 
Our business module is responsible for purchasing, sales, and inventory management of raw materials and managing our partnerships with other organizations.

F35CD96CF35D7192:
INBOX 
The Inbox is used for storing the incoming mail and mail attachments you receive, Endministrator. Expired mail will be automatically deleted, so please check the Inbox often and claim the attachments before they disappear. When the Inbox is full, receiving new incoming mail will also auto-delete the oldest mail. 

INBOX CACHE 
The Inbox Cache is used for storing things that the Endministrator may have missed for a variety of reasons. Items missed due to full storage, inability to pickup the object on time, or other reasons will be placed in the cache. 

The cache can only hold items for limited time so try to pick them up quickly. Items that expire in the cache will permanently disappear. If the cache is full, additional items missed will not be placed in the cache. 

Make sure you have enough storage space when retrieving items from the cache. You cannot retrieve things from the cache if your storage is full.

F37BEAE2EA12DF66:
There are 2 types of Operator skills: area-of-effect (AOE) skills and 1-target skills. 
After choosing the casting direction of AOE skills, all enemies within the AOE will take damage. When casting 1-target skills, you can choose a target for the skill.
F37C8B85B20C99B0:
A special crystal polyhedron manufactured using Techno-Protocol and unconventional crystallization processes. Often combined with other devices to improve physiological absorption of enhancement materials. 
Use this to upgrade the skills of certain Operators.
F380AEFDDEAB352C:
ÆTHERIUM 
While exploring, you may occasionally collect a special item scattered throughout the world known as the Ætherium. You may need to complete certain exploration challenges in order to collect some of them. 

SUBMITTING ÆTHERIUM
You can submit collected Ætherium at any TP Point to get items or boosts that may help in your exploration. Keep in mind that TP Point location does not matter when submitting Ætherium as all TP Points share the same Ætherium progression.

Complete the survey and receive another mail with an attachment of in-game rewards (Oroberyl x50). *This survey mail expires in 1 day to ensure that the feedback is reflective of the actual gaming experience. The reward mail will not expire. Thank you for your understanding. Once again, we're extremely grateful for your continuous support, Endministrator! Enjoy your trip at Talos-II!

F7DD4A07C7B09A50:
Operators who worked with Fjall know that in addition to standard Endfield weaponry, he also carries a unique battle axe. Fjall himself disclosed that he has wielded the axe for many years and is proficient with its use. He treats it as a handy comrade that he will not easily part with. 

After analyzing Fjall's combat trials and daily training performances, many instructors found that Fjall would subconsciously restrain himself when using standard one-handed weapons, limiting the power and speed of his attacks. However, when Fjall wields that special battle axe of his, such limitations will be replaced by near-pathological levels of ferocity. An instructor believes that Fjall should be most proficient dual-wielding those battle axes, and the Fjall we're seeing now is always trying to keep a lid on his impulses and rage during battle. Fjall himself does not deny any of this but emphasizes the necessity of keeping himself in check. His reason is, to quote the man: "A combat surgeon's primary concern is the well-being of his team and offer medical aid in a timely manner." 

"Please join my team! I'll give you the most important assault position on our left wing!" ——a captain of a team (who wishes to remain anonymous) is reported to have said these words at the company canteen in an effort to recruit Operator Fjall. The line is kept on the record by the HR division.

des_e1m2_012: Talk to the repair crew about the situation.
des_e1m2_017: Transmit electricity to the Relay Towers.
des_e1m2_018: Pull the electric cable to the Warehouse Gate.
des_e1m3_001: Enter the Warehouse and find Andre.
des_e1m3_002: Sneak past the Aggeloi and find a way to the upper floor.
des_e1m3_005: Find the room where Andre is hiding at.
des_e1m3_008: Try out the weapons that Chen brought for you.
des_e1m3_009: Discuss with others about your next step.
des_e1m8_001: Find the source of the UWST signal.
des_e1m8_002: Find the source of the UWST signal.
des_e1m8_003: Investigate the source of the signal
des_e2m2_001: Find a way to enter the Research Center.
des_e2m2_002: Talk to people in the camp for intel.
des_e2m2_003: Head to the Peripheral Park main gate.
des_e2m2_004: Find another way into the Peripheral Park.
des_e2m2_007: Find a way through the Peripheral Park.
des_e2m2_008: Find a way to the surface level.
des_e2m2_009: Find a way through the Peripheral Park.
des_e2m2_010: Defeat the enemy and use the back gate to leave the Peripheral Park.
des_e2m6_003: Check the gate of the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_004: Follow the energy readings and find the batteries.
des_e2m6_005: Return to the gate of the Old Mines and use the batteries.
des_e2m6_006: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_007: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_008: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_010: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_012: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_013: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_014: Go deeper into the Old Mines.
des_e2m6_016: Try to repair the gate switch.
des_e2m7_002: Avoid the pulse waves and reach higher grounds.
des_e2m7_003: Go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_004: Go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_006: Avoid the pulse waves and go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_007: Reach higher ground and destroy the Blight Core.
des_e2m7_008: Go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_009: Go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_011: Avoid the pulse waves and go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_012: Avoid the pulse waves and go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m7_014: Avoid the pulse waves and go deeper into the Chasm Zone.
des_e2m8_001: Reach the camp at the intersection.
des_e2m8_003: Go further in and find traces left by Ajimu.
des_side1m9_001: Talk to Fedel when you are ready.
des_side1m9_006: Go to the area with unusual readings.
des_side1m9_008: Return to the Warehouse Gate and talk to Fedel.
des_side2m202_002: Find the Originium Slug mentioned by the one in the crate.
des_side2m202_003: Return to the one in the crate.
des_side2m202_005: Return to the one in the crate.
ep_e1m2_des: To restore order to Base Command, the Endministrator must defeat enemies within the Base and quickly learn how certain facilities work.
ep_e2m4_des: There's a downslope path to the Ankhors. If we can pass through the inactive Blight zone, there will be 2 additional paths for us.
ep_e2m6_des: After acquiring basic intel from Perlica, the Endministrator's team entered an old, abandoned UWST mine and readied themselves to destroy the Ankhors. Nobody expected to see new Originium growths in this area, as the Old Mines were supposed to have dried up...
ep_e2m7_des: After hearing Chen's scouting report, the Endministrator's team entered the severely Blighted Chasm to destroy the Ankhors. The Chasm Area used to be an intersection of multiple highways. Violent changes to the terrain, active Blight, and Ankhor pulses have now made this place extremely hazardous.
ep_e2m9_des: One of the 3 Ankhors is destroyed. The Endministrator's team arrived at an intersection. The road straight ahead is blocked by a mysterious energy barrier. Destroying the Ankhor seems to weaken this barrier... Anyway, just choose one of the 2 paths available to you.
ep_sid_e1m1_des: The Endministrator met a logistics machinist in the Hub Base Logistics Transfer area who seems to have a bit of a problem on his hands...
episode_blc_1_subname: Use episode_tut to check common contents.
giude_group_pushedbox1_2: The Suspended Crate can be freely moved along any 4 directions so long as there's nothing blocking the path. But if you stray too far away, the Crate will return to its starting position.
giude_group_pushedbox2_2: Push the Suspended Crate to the area right above the sensor to activate the switch.
giude_group_pushedbox2_3: Interact with the reset switch to return all Suspended Crates in this stage to their starting positions.
guide_desc_open_statistic_1: The production efficiency screen records changes of production yield and raw material consumption over time for every product.
guide_desc_open_statistic_2: Click the button left of the list to <@gd.key>track</> a product. The tracked product will be <@gd.key>pinned</> to the top of the list and the [AIC Assistant Report] will include its production yield from the previous day.
guide_desc_open_statistic_3: You can also filter and sort products displayed at the bottom of this screen, or edit the period of time of the Production Stats data displayed.
guide_desc_open_statistic_4: This displays electricity production by the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) and electricity consumption of all AIC facilities over a specified time period. Click the bottom of this screen to edit the period of time you wish to check.
guide_desc_open_statistic_5: The AIC Assistant Report gives a summary on the amount of certain products produced in the <@gd.key>last 3 days</>. The items report will also include products that you are <@gd.key>tracking</>. The AIC Assistant will only update the Report <@gd.key>at the end of the day</>.
guide_group_belt_tech1_hint_2_1: Go to any Output Port of the <@gd.key>Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC)</> and select the <@gd.key>Krystallite</>.
guide_group_belt_tech1_hint_2_2: The Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) has a total of <@gd.key>6</> Output Ports, each can output a <@gd.key>different</> material/product.
guide_group_belt_tech1_hint_3: Connect one end of the transport belt to the <@gd.key>Output Port with the Krystallite selected </> and the other to the <@gd.key>Refining Unit</>.
guide_group_belt_tech1_hint_4: Connect the <@gd.key>Output</> of the Refining Unit producing the <@gd.key>Krystallite Fiber</> to the <@gd.key>Input</> of the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC).
guide_group_belt_tech1_hint_5: This completes our basic production line. Go to the <@gd.key>Manufacturing Station</> and start making some <@gd.key>Compact Script Ⅰ</>.
guide_group_bloc1: Once the <@gd.key>Faction system</> is unlocked, you may talk with the faction liaison to learn about your relations with the said faction or purchase supplies from them.
guide_group_bloc2: Improve your relations with a faction and you will receive rewards. Raising relations levels with certain factions will also convince them to sell <@gd.key>rarer supplies</>.
guide_group_bloc3: You must spend <@gd.key>Trade Certs</> to buy supplies from factions. If the supplies are sold out for the day, they will be restocked on the following day.
guide_group_bloc3_title: Faction: Buying and restocking of supplies
guide_group_bloc4: After constructing a <@gd.key>Trading Station</> in your Automated Industry Complex (AIC), you can complete faction orders to raise your relations with them and earn some <@gd.key>Trade Certs</>. Faction liaisons are more than happy to take these Trade Certs in exchange for various faction supplies being offered.
guide_group_camp_intro_1: You may come across <@gd.key>Tele-Protocol Points (TP Points)</> when you explore the wilderness.
guide_group_camp_intro_2: Activated TP Points let you <@gd.key>manage the Depot</> and <@gd.key>rest</>. Operators knocked out of combat will be restored to combat-ready status. All Operators will also <@gd.key>be treated to 50% HP</>.
guide_group_camp_intro_3: When you are at the TP Point, feel free to use the <@gd.key>Manage Depot</> feature to open the [Bag] and place items you don't need right now into the Depot to free up more bag space.
guide_group_camp_intro_4: You can go to the [Map] and select any <@gd.key>TP Point</> to directly Tele-protocol your team to that point.
guide_group_enhance_1: Once the Assembly Station is upgraded to level <@gd.key>4</>, you can use the Assembly Station to perform Artifice Crafting on <@gd.key>Lv.40 or higher gear</>.
guide_group_enhance_2: Once Artifice Crafting is available, every production will <@gd.key>automatically be an Artifice Crafting attempt</>. If successful, <@gd.key>Artifice Points will be spent to produce a piece of Artifice Gear</>, otherwise a normal gear will be produced instead.
guide_group_enhance_3: Artifice Gear has the same set effects as their normal counterparts. Artifice Gear stats have the same contents, but actual stats will depend on the conditions when the Artifice Gear was produced.
guide_group_equip_enhance_1: Once Artifice Crafting is unlocked, producing gear via the <@gd.key>Artifice Mode</> has a chance to spend <@gd.key>Artifice Points</> and produce a piece of Artifice Gear.
guide_group_ether1: When exploring, you might come across <@gd.key>Ætherium</>, a paranormal substance that gives off a strange glow.
guide_group_ether2: Unstable Ætherium might move around, and some can even distort light to make itself invisible. You must <@gd.key>Scan</> the area to verify its presence.
guide_group_ether3: Deliver the <@gd.key>Ætherium</> to the <@gd.key>TP Point</> and get items that may help in your exploration. Don't miss them for anything.
guide_group_ether3_hint: Ætherium submission feature unlocked. You can now go to the <@gd.key>TP Point</> and submit the Ætherium you collected so far.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_1_1: Take the Electric Mining Rig to an <@gd.key>Originium mining spot</> near the Base.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_1_2: REMINDER! The Electric Mining Rig must be <@gd.key>connected to the electrical grid</> to start working. Don't forget to use <@gd.key>Electric Pylons</> and deliver electricity to the Rig.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_2: First dismantle the <@gd.key>Originium Rig</> you placed earlier, then place the <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig</> on the mining spot, and connect the new Rig to the electrical grid.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_3: Place the <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig</> on the mining spot and connect the Rig to the electrical grid.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_4: Connect the <@gd.key>Terminal Pylon</> at the entrance of the Southwest Logistics Transfer Center to the electrical grid to restore the supply of electricity.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_5: Place the <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig</> on the mining spot.
guide_group_miner2_intro_text_6: The <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig</> is working smoothly. The production line is complete.
guide_group_moon_1: A rare and stable manifestation of Ætherside matter. Get more <@gd.key>Æther Manifest</> to raise the Protocol Capacity of this area. Facilities constructed outside the Core AIC Area will use up Protocol Capacity, so having more Capacity will let you construct more facilities.
guide_group_start_bomb1_desc_1: We can create explosives using barrels containing explosive materials. Adjust the camera and aiming guide, then click the [Attack] button to toss the explosive that will then explode after a few seconds. Fortunately, they <@gd.key>do not</> inflict friendly damage.
guide_group_start_bomb1_desc_2: Explosives have limited stability and tend to sublimate completely when you move too far away from the barrel of explosive materials. Don't worry. Just wait for a few seconds and you'll be able to make another.
guide_group_tempshop_01: We unlocked the <@gd.key>Exchange</> feature.<@gd.key>This feature is only available for this Technical Test</>.
guide_group_tempshop_02: Press the hotkey <sprite name=esc> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here to enter the <@gd.key>Protosync</>. You should be able to see the <@gd.key>Exchange</>.
guide_group_tempshop_03: The <@gd.key>Exchange</> lets us spend <@gd.key>Oroberyls</> to buy rare weapons and Operator Recruitment Certs.
guide_group_travelpole_intro_text_1: Place a <@gd.key>Zipline Pylon connected to the electrical grid</> in the Core AIC Area.
guide_group_travelpole_intro_text_2: Place the <@gd.key>Zipline Pylon</> in the Bag.
guide_group_travelpole_intro_text_3: Bring the <@gd.key>Zipline Pylon</> and head to the resource-rich area west of the Base Depot.
guide_group_travelpole_intro_text_4_1: Place another <@gd.key>Zipline Pylon</> in the resource area and connect it to the electrical grid.
guide_group_travelpole_intro_text_4_2: REMINDER! The 2 Zipline Pylons <@gd.key>must be within 80 meters of each other with nothing between them</> to create a zipline.
guide_group_wiki2: As you explore the world, the EFDB will continue to register tutorials, collectibles, and other key information you come across.
guide_group_wiki3: Use hotkey <sprite name=k> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here and peruse these contents.
guide_text_ap_1: Exploring and salvaging resources from Rifts will consume <@gd.key>Sanity</>, so it is best to plan the use of Sanity.
guide_text_ap_2: Reduced <@gd.key>Sanity</> may affect you. When <@gd.key>Sanity</> reduction is beneath threshold, your entry into the Rift may be interrupted.
guide_text_ap_3: You will naturally recover spent <@gd.key>Sanity</> with time, but reaching Max Sanity ends natural Sanity recovery. We recommend exploring other Rifts and salvage resources from there.
guide_text_bag_full_1: If your Bag is full, you won't be able to acquire new items. Consider going to the TP Point safe zone to move extra items to the <@gd.key>Depot</> or <@gd.key>destroy</> items you don't need.
guide_text_bandwidth_des_1:
Every area has its own <@gd.key>Protocol Capacity</>. Every facility constructed outside the Core AIC Area will take up its specified amounts of Protocol Capacity. 
Once Protocol Capacity is maxed, no more facilities can be placed in the same area.
guide_text_bandwidth_des_2: Collecting <@gd.key>Æther Manifest</> of the area can raise the Protocol Capacity of the same area.
guide_text_belt_connect2_1: Excellent work. We have now selected Krystallite as the output material for this Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) <@gd.key>Output Port</>.
guide_text_belt_connect2_2: Now, use hotkey <sprite name=e> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the transport belt quick button and place the transport belts.
guide_text_belt_connect_1: Good job. We now have plenty of Krystallite and Originium Ore in the 2 Stashes.
guide_text_belt_connect_2: Click to close and leave the Stash operations screen.
guide_text_belt_connect_3: Now, use hotkey <sprite name=e> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the transport belt quick button and place the transport belts.
guide_text_belt_connect_des2_1: Click the starting point. Move and rotate the screen to navigate and connect to the end point and create a transport belt route.
guide_text_belt_connect_des2_2: Let's connect the PAC <@gd.key>Krystallite Output Port to the Refining Unit</> to create an automated production line.
guide_text_belt_connect_des_1: Click the starting point. Move and rotate the screen to navigate and connect to the end point and create a transport belt route.
guide_text_belt_connect_des_2: Now let's <@gd.key>connect the Stash to the Refining Unit</> to create an automated production line.
guide_text_belt_del_1: Click here to collect materials from this transport belt grid.
guide_text_belt_del_2: OR click here to collect materials from the entire length of this transport belt.
guide_text_belt_del_3: Click here to stash this transport belt <@gd.key>grid unit</>.
guide_text_belt_del_4: OR click here to stash the <@gd.key>entire length</> of the transport belt in one go.
guide_text_belt_intro_des_1: <@gd.key>Transport belts</> are essential to any AIC logistics infrastructure. They are responsible for moving items between 2 facilities connected by the <@gd.key>transport belts</>. If you have a production line with multiple processing steps, <@gd.key>transport belts</> can save you the hassle of going back and forth between the buildings.
guide_text_belt_intro_des_2: Use the hotkey <sprite name=e> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the transport belt quick button to enter <@gd.key>Logistics Mode</>. All facilities that can be connected by transport belts will display input or output arrows that indicate how the belts can be connected.
guide_text_belt_intro_des_3: <@gd.key>Connect one end of the transport belt to the output of Facility A and then connect it to the input of Facility B</>. Items produced by Facility A will then endlessly stream along the belt to the Input Cache of Facility B. Setup the transport belt connections properly to set up a fully automated production line.
guide_text_bloc_mission_1: Now let's give you a simple introduction of the <@gd.key>Area Stability Project</>.
guide_text_bloc_mission_2: We will often receive tasks posted by other factions. Completing these tasks can improve the <@gd.key>Stability</> of the local area.
guide_text_bloc_mission_3: Different factions will post different tasks. You can only attempt 1 task at any given time.
guide_text_bloc_mission_4: Let's open the [Task] panel to see what's available.
guide_text_bloc_mission_5: While viewing tasks posted by each faction, you can also use refresh to see if there are different tasks available.
guide_text_bloc_mission_6: You can go to the <@gd.key>Task Terminal</> to abandon a task you have right now.
guide_text_bloc_mission_7: There is a daily limit to the number of tasks you can accept and complete. Complete all the tasks to get a bonus progression reward.
guide_text_bloc_mission_8: You can also conveniently check Area Stability Project information at various [TP Points].
guide_text_building_cache_des_1: You can drag items in/out of your Bag or Depot to the Input Cache of the facility. If a proper formula exists, then the facility will start operations.
guide_text_building_cache_des_2: An Input Cache can at most have 4 item slots. Each item type can only take up 1 slot and each slot can only hold a limited number of the item.
guide_text_building_cache_des_3:
You can only drag items OUT of a facility's Output Cache but NOT IN. 
Items in the Output Cache can be dragged into your Bag or Depot.
guide_text_building_cache_title_1: Moving items into the Input Cache
guide_text_building_cache_title_3: Moving items out of the Output Cache
guide_text_camp_01: We have activated a <@gd.key>TP Point</>. An activated TP Point will offer various features and amenities.
guide_text_camp_02: Use the <@gd.key>rest</> amenities to restore Operators knocked out of combat back to combat-ready status. All Operators will also be <@gd.key>treated to 50% HP</>.
guide_text_camp_03: Standing within the safe zone of the <@gd.key>TP Point</> links you to the <@gd.key>Endfield Depot Management System</>. The System lets you manage items in your current Bag and the Depot.
guide_text_camp_04: You can also use hotkey <sprite name=b> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the target icon to open the Bag and start managing Depot at once.
guide_text_camp_05: In Depot Management mode, use the [Stash All] button to move everything from your Bag into the Depot.
guide_text_camp_06: <@gd.key>Filters</> can also be applied on Stash All for some smart item moving. Let's click the [Filter] button.
guide_text_camp_07: By default, most common categories will not be selected. [Usable goods] is a common category, and includes items like <@gd.key>Buckflower Powder</>.
guide_text_camp_08: Click anywhere to close the filter screen.
guide_text_camp_10: <@gd.key>Checked</> items in our Bag will be moved to the Depot with [Stash All]. You can head to any safe zone (<@gd.key>near the TP Points</>) to interact with your Bag and Depot again.
guide_text_capsmode_desc_1:
In AIC Mode, use the hotkey <sprite name=capslock> to enter <@gd.key>top view</>. 
Top view mode gives you a better look at the entire AIC area and perform some actions with better precision.
guide_text_char_equip_1: We acquired new pieces of gear. Let's equip them.
guide_text_char_equip_2: Use hotkey <sprite name=c> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here to open the Operator Overview.
guide_text_char_equip_3: This shows you the current gear equipped by the Operator and detailed stats. You can also replace equipped gear as required.
guide_text_char_equip_4: This screen displays the gear currently equipped by the Operator. Click the gear slot to replace that specific gear piece. You can also use the [Switch] feature at the lower right to replace equipped gear.
guide_text_char_equip_5: This displays information of gear currently equipped by the Operator. Equipping multiple pieces of gear from the same set activates <@gd.key>set effects</>, so try to experiment with different gear combinations.
guide_text_char_equip_6: Now let's check out the piece of gear we just acquired.
guide_text_char_equip_7: This list shows all the pieces of gear we currently have.
guide_text_char_equip_8: Any piece of gear can be <@gd.key>equipped</> or <@gd.key>unequipped</>.
guide_text_char_equip_9: Feel free to adjust your Operator's loadout of equipped gear according to what they need.
guide_text_char_exp_1: In addition to experience items, you can also earn experience by <@gd.key>defeating enemies</> during exploration.
guide_text_char_exp_2: However, once Operators <@gd.key>reach Level 20</>, they will need to use different experience items. Defeating enemies will also no longer give experience.
guide_text_char_exp_3: Carefully monitor Operator levels and plan out your leveling accordingly.
guide_text_char_levelup_1: To ensure victory in increasingly difficult battles, we must increase our effectiveness in combat, Endministrator.
guide_text_char_levelup_2: Use hotkey <sprite name=c> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here to open the Operator Overview.
guide_text_char_levelup_3: If the Operator is below the level cap, this is the portal to the <@gd.key>level up</> screen.
guide_text_char_levelup_4: Consuming items of this category can give Operators experience and level them up.
guide_text_char_levelup_5: When an Operator reaches a certain level, they must <@gd.key>use other experience items</> to keep leveling up.
guide_text_char_levelup_6: If the Operator hits a level cap, they must undergo <@gd.key>Promotion</> to raise their level cap and keep leveling up.
guide_text_char_stageup_1: You now have a Level 20 Operator who has reached their current level cap. The Operator must be <@gd.key>promoted</> before they can be leveled up again.
guide_text_char_stageup_2: Promotion enhances an Operator's skills and may even unlock new ones.
guide_text_char_stageup_3: After reaching Level 20, <@gd.key>Operators can no longer gain experience by defeating monsters</>, but they can use various Compact Scripts to keep leveling up.
guide_text_cmpt_mc_intro_desc_1:
The <@gd.key>Fitting Unit</> can process materials with the [Fit] tag, making products often used in facilities or final fitting. 
Typical output: <@gd.key>Krystallite Part</>.
guide_text_connector_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Belt Bridge</> allows a transport belt to go over another without disrupting the flow of items of both belts.
guide_text_connector_intro_desc_2: You can go to the Facility List to select the <@gd.key>Belt Bridge</>. You can either place it directly or drag it into the hotbar.
guide_text_control_move: Use the <sprite name=w><sprite name=a><sprite name=s><sprite name=d> keys to move around.
guide_text_control_observe: Move the mouse to look around.
guide_text_converger_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Converger</> can converge up to 3 transport belts into a single belt.
guide_text_converger_intro_desc_2: You can go to the Facility List to select the <@gd.key>Converger</>. You can either place it directly or drag it into the hotbar.
guide_text_dead_hint1_1: <@gd.key>Use Crafting to craft gear</> and level up your Operators to make battles easier.
guide_text_dead_hint2_1: <@gd.key>Challenge the Rift to earn resources</> and improve your team's effectiveness.
guide_text_dead_hint3_1: Research AIC technologies to <@gd.key>produce better gear at the Assembly Station</> and improve your team's effectiveness.
guide_text_doodad_intro_des_1: Talos-II is rich in gatherable resources. Some might be available after giving it an attack or two.
guide_text_doodad_intro_des_2: Some resources can be directly gathered, while some must be attacked a few times to become gatherable.
guide_text_doodad_intro_des_3: Perform Scan actions in unknown areas as often as you can to uncover new resources.
guide_text_dungeon_1: Now let's give you a simple introduction of the <@gd.key>Rift Space</>.
guide_text_dungeon_2: Every <@gd.key>Rift Space</> has been named and tagged accordingly to avoid confusion and inform you of the dangers within.
guide_text_dungeon_3: Information on enemies within the <@gd.key>Rift Space</> has been recorded as intel.
guide_text_dungeon_4: Information on salvageable resources within the <@gd.key>Rift Space</> has been recorded as well. Refer to what you need and select the proper Rift Space to explore.
guide_text_enemy_break: An enemy enters the Weakened status when their Stability is broken. Such enemy suffers greatly increased damage.
guide_text_enemy_elite: Stability gives damage reduction. Use skills to whittle down and break their Stability to inflict Weakened upon them.
guide_text_enemy_interrupt_0: Enemies may enter the Charging status as they prepare to cast a powerful skill (a <@gd.key>shrinking red circle</> will appear around them to warn you).
guide_text_enemy_interrupt_1: At this time, you can use <@gd.key>control skills (such as those that inflict Knocked Down, Lifted, or Knocked Back)</> to <@gd.key>interrupt</> the enemy skill casting and deal serious damage.
guide_text_enemy_interrupt_2: Interrupting the enemy stops them from casting the skill and <@gd.key>also breaks their Stability</>.
guide_text_enemy_poise_intro_1: Almost all enemies have <@gd.key>Stability</>. Breaking enemy Stability will swing the battle in your favor.
guide_text_enemy_poise_intro_2: Every skill can break Stability. Enemies <@gd.key>hit by a skill</> takes some Stability DMG.
guide_text_enemy_poise_window_1: When enemy Stability is reduced to ZERO, they will enter the <@gd.key>Break</> status.
guide_text_enemy_poise_window_2: Broken enemies will be force-<@gd.key>stunned</> for several seconds and take <@gd.key>50% more</> damage for the duration of the Break.
guide_text_enemy_poise_window_3: Powerful enemies have more Stability, so plan your moves accordingly.
guide_text_enemy_skill: Elite enemies have rather powerful skills. You must either evade those skills or cast your own skills to interrupt their skill casting.
guide_text_enemyarmor_desc_1: Some powerful enemies (such as Cliff) has a constant <@gd.key>Mantle</> effect (indicated by the <@gd.key>Gold Shield</> icon). Your Operators <@gd.key>cannot</> deal control effects (such as Lifted or Knocked Down) upon enemies with an active Mantle.
guide_text_enemyarmor_desc_2: However, enemies with Mantle are NOT invincible. While they cast Charging skills (indicated by the <@gd.key>red circle</> around them), inflict control effects (such as Lifted or Knocked Down) upon them to temporarily <@gd.key>remove</> their Mantle effect.
guide_text_enemyarmor_desc_3: Once the Mantle is removed from the enemy, use the opportunity and unleash as much damage as you can.
guide_text_energy_intro_0: Operator and enemy skills may create <@gd.key>Elemental Orbs</> in the battlefield. Cast area-of-effect (AOE) skills to trigger these Elemental Orbs to <@gd.key>unleash special effects</>.
guide_text_energy_intro_01: The Endministrator has <@gd.key>Pulse</> elemental skills. When this skill comes in contact with <@gd.key>Pulse Orbs</>, a <@gd.key>Shock</> effect is triggered, dealing damage and Stability reduction to a large area.
guide_text_energy_intro_1: Operator and enemy skills may create Elemental Orbs in the battlefield. These Orbs can be used to trigger special effects.
guide_text_energy_intro_2: Cast area-of-effect (AOE) skills to trigger these Elemental Orbs. Different elemental combinations will trigger different effects.
guide_text_energy_intro_n: That's it. Triggering <@gd.key>Elemental Orbs</> with different elemental skills will unleash various <@gd.key>powerful effects</>. Use this as much as you can in battle.
guide_text_eswitch_attacked_1: A reactive device temporarily changes its operation mode when attacked or hit by a blast wave.
guide_text_fac_field_intro_des_1:
We now have a bigger Core AIC Area that gives us more space for facilities. 
Placing facilities in this Area will <@gd.key>NOT use up any Protocol Capacity</>.
guide_text_fac_unlocker_desc_1:
Endfield has deployed a <@gd.key>Sector Scanner</> at an appropriate location. 
Endministrator, find and activate the <@gd.key>Sector Scanner</> to acquire scan data of the nearby areas.
guide_text_fac_unlocker_desc_2: Acquiring scan data also unlocks facility construction authority levels for the scanned area and provides basic Protocol Capacity. This will then let you deploy facilities in the area.
guide_text_facarea_intro_des_1: Facilities like the <@gd.key>Refining Unit</> and <@gd.key>Shredding Unit</> can only be placed in the Core AIC Area. This information can be viewed in the facility details provided.
guide_text_facgemfusion_1: After defeating enemies and acquiring items known as the sequence, we can head back to the Assembly Station and use this item to complete our research for the corresponding Essence Formula.
guide_text_facgemfusion_2: First, select the <@gd.key>quality you wish to fuse</>. The Assembly Station lets you fuse Essences of 3 qualities: <@gd.key>Blue / Purple / Gold</>. You must start fusing <@gd.key>lower quality essences to steadily acquire higher quality essences</>.
guide_text_facgemfusion_3: For example, you can select 3 <@gd.key>green Essences of the same name</> as the material to fuse into a higher quality <@gd.key>blue Essence of the same name</>.
guide_text_facgemfusion_4: As a reminder, fusing Essences to get the best quality gold Essence also requires the addition of <@gd.key>Essence Softener</>. You can get Essence Softeners by <@gd.key>challenging the Rift</>.
guide_text_facgemfusion_5: You can also use the Recast Essence feature to consume <@gd.key>Essence of the same name</> and get <@gd.key>Essence of the same name and quality</>.
guide_text_facgemfusion_title_1: Assembly Station feature: Fuse Essence I
guide_text_facgemfusion_title_2: Assembly Station feature: Fuse Essence II
guide_text_facgemfusion_title_3: Assembly Station feature: Fuse Essence III
guide_text_facgemfusion_title_4: Assembly Station feature: Fuse Essence IV
guide_text_farmland_intro_des_1: Test Plots can be used to grow <@gd.key>plants, fungus, or vitrean growths</>. The AIC can't produce certain crops, so we must grow them on these plots.
guide_text_farmland_intro_des_2: While gathering plants during exploration, you have a chance to receive <@gd.key>plant seeds, fungus spores, or vitrean rhizomes</>.
guide_text_farmland_intro_des_3: Select the <@gd.key>plant seed, fungus spore, or vitrean rhizome</> to plant into the Plot. The crops will grow and mature after a while, allowing you to harvest them.
guide_text_filling_pd_mc_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Filling Unit</> can process materials with the [Fill] tag and can produce various meds or processed food. Typical output: <@gd.key>G1 Recovery Cocktail</>.
guide_text_finish_techtree_1: We have completed this research node and can now use the Construction Terminal (CON-Term) to construct <@gd.key>Electric Pylons</>!
guide_text_finish_techtree_2: Keep going down the tech tree and research various nodes to support your exploration efforts.
guide_text_finish_techtree_3: This is the recommended research you should start next. Of course, you can definitely research another tech of your choosing.
guide_text_finish_techtree_4: For example, your next primary goal is to research the <@gd.key>Belt Basics</> tech to automate your AIC.
guide_text_finish_techtree_5: We recommend upgrading and expanding your AIC and explore the areas around the Protocol Testing Base. This will get you adequately prepared before you explore more dangerous areas.
guide_text_gemfusion_1: Once the <@gd.key>Fuse Essence</> feature is unlocked, the <@gd.key>Assembly Station</> will let us produce higher quality Essences.
guide_text_gemfusion_2: Essence quality is divided into <@gd.key>4 tiers, in ascending order: green, blue, purple, and gold</>. Fusing <@gd.key>consumes lower quality Essences to produce higher quality Essences</>.
guide_text_gemfusion_3: For example, you can select <@gd.key>green Essences of the same name</> as the starting materials and fuse them into a <@gd.key>blue Essence of the same name</>.
guide_text_gemfusion_4: You can also use the <@gd.key>Recast Essence</> feature to recast unwanted Essences into new Essences.
guide_text_gemfusion_5: Recasting consumes <@gd.key>Essence of the same name</> and gives 1 <@gd.key>Essence of the same name and same quality</>.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_1: We have unlocked the <@gd.key>Fuse Essence</> feature and can now make higher quality Essences. Click here to start.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_1_1: After defeating the enemy and acquiring their sequences, we can head back to the Assembly Station to complete our research on the Essence Formula.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_1_2: Click [Confirm]. Let's now take a look at how Fuse Essence and Recast Essence are done.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_2: We now must select the target quality of our Fuse Essence action. Essence quality is divided into <@gd.key>4 tiers, in ascending order: green, blue, purple, and gold</>. Fusing must <@gd.key>consume lower quality Essences to produce higher quality Essences</>.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_3: For example, you can select <@gd.key>green Essences of the same name</> as the starting materials and fuse them into a <@gd.key>blue Essence of the same name</>.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_4: Click here, then select <@gd.key>3 Essences of the same name of lower quality</> as the Fuse Essence materials.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_5: You can click the [Auto Place] button here to select low quality Essences <@gd.key>not applied on any weapon</>.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_6: Then click the <@gd.key>Start</> button to fuse the selected Essences and get a higher quality Essence.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_7: Now let's take a look at the <@gd.key>Recast Essence</> feature. Click here first.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_8: Recasting consumes an <@gd.key>Essence of the same name</> as the material. Consider using an Essence that you don't need.
guide_text_gemfusion_op_9: Click [Start] and you will receive 1 <@gd.key>Essence of the same name and quality</>.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_1: Use hotkey <sprite name=b> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> to open the Bag.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_10: I can now send you the approximate coordinates for the <@gd.key>Mossfield Wood</> and <@gd.key>Originium Ore</>. Gather some of each to craft the gear.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_3: You can see that Crafting now has the <@gd.key>Gear</> crafting section.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_5: Click the <@gd.key>Basic Explorer Gloves</> and check its crafting requirements.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_6: Crafting it requires <@gd.key>Mossfield Wood</> x5 and <@gd.key>Origocrust</> x5.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_8: Click here to view details on the <@gd.key>Origocrust</>.
guide_text_hdwork_equip_9: Turns out that Crafting also lets you process basic minerals such as <@gd.key>Originium Ore</> into <@gd.key>Origocrust</>.
guide_text_hdwork_formula_intro_des_1: Get new Crafting formulas by acquiring <@gd.key>Templates</>. Finding <@gd.key>certain new materials</> during exploration also unlocks certain Crafting formulas.
guide_text_hdwork_formula_intro_des_2: Try to get as many formulas as you can to support your exploration efforts.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_1: <@gd.key>Crafting</> is now available. Use hotkey <sprite name=b> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the icon to open the Bag.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_2: Go to your Bag window and open the <@gd.key>Crafting</> window.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_3: This feature lets you craft a few basic items. Click here to swap the formula.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_4: <@gd.key>Buckflower Powder</> can help treat wounds. Let's grind the Buckflower we just gathered into <@gd.key>Buckflower Powder</>.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_6: Click [Add to queue] to place the required items or materials into the standby queue.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_7: Once production of an item has been added to the standby queue, said production will continue in the background even if you close the Crafting window.
guide_text_hdwork_medic_hint_1: Go to Bag to access Crafting and make more <@gd.key>Buckflower Powder</>.
guide_text_hint_equip1: Go to the AIC Management Plan and research the <@gd.key>3D Assembly II</> tech to assemble better gear.
guide_text_hint_equip2: You can now go to the AIC Management Plan to research the <@gd.key>3D Assembly III</> tech to assemble better gear and unlock the Fuse Essence feature.
guide_text_hint_facarea_teleport: You can use hotkey <sprite name=m> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> to open the Map screen and find the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) and <@gd.key>quick travel</> to the Core AIC Area directly.
guide_text_hint_full_1: Your Depot now has huge stockpiles of certain products.
guide_text_hint_full_2: If a product reaches the Depot storage limit of 10000, the product can no longer be placed in the Depot. You can move part of the stockpile to other facilities for processing.
guide_text_hint_inside_qkbar: The <@gd.key>Refining Unit</> and <@gd.key>Shredding Unit</> have been automatically added to the hotbar. You can now use the hotkey to deploy them quickly.
guide_text_hint_inside_qkbar_2: Please note that you get separate hotbars for <@gd.key>inside</> and <@gd.key>outside the Core AIC Area</>. Production facilities such as the <@gd.key>Refining Unit</> and <@gd.key>Shredding Unit</> cannot be built outside the Core AIC Area.
guide_text_hint_mail_lost_item:
Alert! Your Bag has no available item slots and you've been forced to leave some items behind. 
Go to the main screen and click the <@gd.key>Inbox</> button (upper right row) to recover the items you missed.
guide_text_hint_medic1: You can also produce <@gd.key>Recovery Cocktail</> in the AIC to help you in battles against powerful enemies.
guide_text_hint_medic2: You can now produce <@gd.key>Recovery Cocktail of higher grades</>. Bring some along in your fight against powerful enemies.
guide_text_hint_mfg_place: The <@gd.key>Manufacturing Station</> beacon is now placed in your Bag. Open your Bag and select the <@gd.key>Manufacturing Station</> to deploy the beacon.
guide_text_hint_mfg_place_2: <@gd.key>Specialized facilities</> like the Manufacturing Station do not need to be constructed in the Construction Terminal (CON-Term). They will be available once unlocked and can start operations <@gd.key>without being connected to the electrical grid</>.
guide_text_hint_move_1: To move a deployed facility, click <@gd.key>[Move]</> and you can move it to a desired location. You can also press the <sprite name=r> key to rotate the facility and adjust the facings of its input and output ports.
guide_text_hint_move_2: Click <@gd.key>[Stash]</> to directly place the facility in the Bag or Depot. <@gd.key>You will not lose anything within facilities when stashing them</>.
guide_text_hint_opminer: Stand close to the <@gd.key>Originium Rig</> and click it to open the details window. You can then take the mined Originium Ore from its cache.
guide_text_hint_power_line: A Relay Tower cannot connect to any facility outside its max cable length of 80 meters. Place another Relay Tower and connect to it to extend the transmission range.
guide_text_hint_power_pole: An Electric Pylon must be <@gd.key>connected to the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC)</> to start transmitting power and supply electricity to other facilities.
guide_text_hint_ppole1_qkbar: Like other facilities, <@gd.key>Electric Pylons</> can also be dragged to the hotbar to make deployment easier.
guide_text_hint_procsta: Click to craft an <@gd.key>Active PPE Suit I</>.
guide_text_hint_storager_place: The <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</> beacon is stored here. Click the <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</> to place it.
guide_text_hint_storager_place_2: Click the <@gd.key>[Place]</> button to place the facility beacon and start facility construction.
guide_text_hint_travelpole_3: The first and second Zipline Pylons must be able to form an <@gd.key>unobstructed connection within 80 meters between them</>. Both must be connected to the electrical grid to create a zipline that you can use.
guide_text_hint_travelpole_desc_1:
Ziplines created by Zipline Pylons <@gd.key>have an 80-meter length limit</>. 
You can successfully create a zipline with 2 Zipline Pylons capable of forming an unobstructed connection within 80 meters between them.
guide_text_hint_travelpole_desc_2:
If a Zipline Pylon is placed outside the <@gd.key>80-meter radius of another, or if something is blocking the way</>, the Zipline Pylons will be unable to form the zipline. 
When placing a Zipline Pylon, try checking how it connects to the others.
guide_text_hint_wiki_unlock_1: Additional details on <@gd.key>weapons and essences</> have been unlocked in the Endfield Database.
guide_text_hint_wiki_unlock_2: Additional details on <@gd.key>Thermal elemental fusions</> have been unlocked in the Endfield Database.
guide_text_hint_wiki_unlock_3: Additional details on <@gd.key>Lattice elemental fusions</> have been unlocked in the Endfield Database.
guide_text_hint_wiki_unlock_4: Additional details on <@gd.key>placing logistic facilities</> have been unlocked in the Endfield Database.
guide_text_hint_wiki_unlock_5: Additional items on <@gd.key>Lifted statuses and channeling skills</> have been unlocked for the Endfield Database.
guide_text_hint_workshop_place: The <@gd.key>Construction Terminal</> beacon is stored here. Click the <@gd.key>Construction Terminal</> to place it.
guide_text_hub_plink_1: We just got some new <@gd.key>Relay Towers</> and can now transmit power from the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) to the Warehouse.
guide_text_hub_plink_des_1: The <@gd.key>Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC)</> is the only source terminal of electricity. Any power-consuming facilities must be connected to the  <@gd.key>PAC</> directly or indirectly via Electric Pylons or Relay Towers to work.
guide_text_hub_storage_1: The <@gd.key>Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC)</> is repaired. Our Core AIC Area can now be treated as a safe zone.
guide_text_hub_storage_2: Let's use hotkey <sprite name=b> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> to open the Bag and Depot.
guide_text_hub_storage_3: Let's click the [Stash All] button. This moves every item in our Bag to the Depot with a single click.
guide_text_hub_storage_4: In the Core AIC Area, you can also use hotkey <sprite name=m> to enter the Mini Map and teleport to other places. You can also use the Mini Map to teleport back to the Core AIC Area.
guide_text_hub_unloader_1: The PAC has a total of 6 Output Ports, <@gd.key>each can output a different material/product</>.
guide_text_hub_unloader_2: Let's click this Output Port and select the material/product to be moved out of the PAC.
guide_text_hub_unloader_4: Let's select Krystallite as the output material of this <@gd.key>this Output Port</>.
guide_text_hub_unloader_5: Now we can connect one end of a transport belt to this Output Port and the other end to a facility. If the Depot has available Krystallite, the transport belt will <@gd.key>automatically deliver</> the material to the connected facility!
guide_text_item_tag_des_1:
Most items that can be handled by the Automated Industry Complex (AIC) carry a tag. 
When the tag of a particular item matches that of a facility, then that item can be part of that facility's production process.
guide_text_item_tag_des_2:
In addition to facility formulas, checking item properties is also an easy way to verify the production processes that the item can be a part of. 
Once a new facility is researched, new tags might appear for an item, making that item usable for the specified production facility.
