guide_text_log_connector_hint_1: Use hotkey <sprite name=q> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the Facility List to select the <@gd.key>Belt Bridge</> and other logistics facilities to place in the Core AIC Area.
guide_text_log_converger_hint_1: Use hotkey <sprite name=q> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the Facility List to select the <@gd.key>Converger</> and other logistics facilities to place in the Core AIC Area.
guide_text_log_splitter_hint_1: Use hotkey <sprite name=q> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the Facility List to select the <@gd.key>Splitter</> and other logistics facilities to place in the Core AIC Area.
guide_text_logistic_forward_des_1: After researching the <@gd.key>Belt Basics</> tech, input and output ports will appear for facilities in the Core AIC Area.
guide_text_logistic_forward_des_2: Ports can be connected to each other via <@gd.key>transport belts</> to set up automatic production lines and product delivery.
guide_text_logistic_forward_title_1: Materials, transport belts, and automation I
guide_text_logistic_forward_title_2: Materials, transport belts, and automation II
guide_text_mail_lost_item_1: Items you could not collect due to full storage will be recovered by the field recovery team and sent to your <@gd.key>Inbox Cache</>. Click the <@gd.key>Inbox</> button in the main screen (upper right row) to check it out.
guide_text_mail_lost_item_2:
Click this tab to view the items you missed. 
You must have available space in your <@gd.key>Bag</> or <@gd.key>Valuables Stash</> to claim these missed items.
guide_text_make_furnance_1: This is the Construction Terminal (CON-Term) screen. The <@gd.key>CON-Term</> is literally a machine that constructs other machines. This is the starting point of all Automated Industry Complex (AIC) facilities of Endfield Industries.
guide_text_make_furnance_2: Let's first switch to the [PROD I] tab for basic productions.
guide_text_make_furnance_3: Select the <@gd.key>Refining Unit</> to create our first key facility.
guide_text_make_furnance_5: We are about to get another <@gd.key>Refining Unit</>.
guide_text_make_grinder_2: Follow the steps we took for constructing the <@gd.key>Refining Unit</>, except that we're choosing the <@gd.key>Shredding Unit</> this time.
guide_text_make_grinder_4: We are about to get another <@gd.key>Shredding Unit</>.
guide_text_make_grinder_5: Click here to leave the Construction Terminal (CON-Term).
guide_text_medic_sta_intro_desc_1: Once constructed, connect the <@gd.key>Medical Arts Tower</> to a source of electricity to charge the facility. The Medical Arts Tower converts stored electricity into Medical Arts reserves. The Medical Arts Tower will treat any injured personnel who approach until it runs out of Medical Arts reserves.
guide_text_medic_sta_intro_desc_2: If every Operator in the team is already at <@gd.key>Max HP</> and the Medical Arts Tower still has reserves left, it will begin to restore the Operator's <@gd.key>Skill Point (SP)</> until it runs out of Medical Arts reserves.
guide_text_miner2_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig</> can mine additional types of minerals, but they must be connected to a source of electricity to work.
guide_text_miner2_intro_desc_2: The <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig</> has belt-free transfer features that automatically deliver mined ores from its cache to the Depot!
guide_text_miner3_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rig Mk II</> is much improved and capable of mining harder ores such as the Oriron Ore.
guide_text_miner_intro_des_1: Mining Rigs can only be placed on mining spots.
guide_text_miner_intro_des_2: Higher tier minerals cannot be mined by low tier mining rigs.
guide_text_miner_intro_des_3: The <@gd.key>Originium Rig</> is special as it is self-sufficient in energy and can endlessly mine Originium Ore <@gd.key>without being connected to a source of electricity</>.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_1: The <@gd.key>Assignment Management</> screen lets you easily <@gd.key>assign</> Operators to various AIC facilities.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_10: Close the Assignment Management screen and head back to the AIC Mode main screen to see how it works.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_2: Click the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) and select the Operators to assign to the PAC.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_3: Operators have their special <@gd.key>AIC Skills</>. Click on the Operator to check those skills.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_4: Most AIC Skills only take effect when the Operator is assigned to a <@gd.key>matching facility</> such as the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC).
guide_text_open_char_garrison_5: If the Operator is assigned to the right facility, the AIC Skill will <@gd.key>glow</> and take effect.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_6: Click [Save Settings] and assign the idling Operator to the PAC.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_7: You can assign up to 3 Operators to each special facility. Try to assign Operators to the right facility whenever possible to improve AIC performance and efficiency.
guide_text_open_char_garrison_8: Try using the <@gd.key>pin</> feature. Click the pin-shaped icon at the bottom of the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC).
guide_text_open_char_garrison_9: Pinned special facilities will appear as a <@gd.key>quick portal</> on the right side of the AIC Mode main screen. You can then click that to open the facility details screen without having to walk over to the facility itself.
guide_text_open_furnance_1: Once the factory details window is open, we can click here to open your storage.
guide_text_open_furnance_2: The storage window is displayed to the left of the screen. The Bag is the one being opened right now. The Refining Unit production screen is displayed on the right. You can drag items from the storage to the production <@gd.key>Input Cache</>.
guide_text_open_furnance_3: The Refining Unit can <@gd.key>refine</> any material that can be smelted down. Let's try having the Refining Unit process the <@gd.key>Originium Ore</>.
guide_text_open_furnance_4: Drag the Originium Ore from the Bag to the <@gd.key>Input Cache</> of the Refining Unit.
guide_text_open_furnance_5: The previously idle Refining Unit is now in production and will process raw materials into products over time. Finished products will be placed in the Output Cache to await pickup.
guide_text_open_furnance_6: Once enough has been produced, you can move the products from the Output Cache to your Bag or Depot.
guide_text_open_furnance_hint1: This Refining Unit is not connected to the electrical grid. It must have electricity to start operating.
guide_text_open_furnance_hint2: You have no Originium Ore right now. Gather some from the wilderness before returning to the Refining Unit.
guide_text_open_furnance_s1: Click here to switch to the <@gd.key>Depot</>.
guide_text_open_furnance_s2: You can also drag items from the Depot to the <@gd.key>Input Cache</>.
guide_text_open_furnance_s3: Let's switch back to the <@gd.key>Bag</> first.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_1: Once the factory details window is open, we can click here to open your storage.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_2: The storage window is displayed to the left of the screen. The Bag is the one being opened right now. The Refining Unit production screen is displayed on the right.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_3: Click here to switch to the Depot window.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_4: The Depot has separate tabs for each category of items. Let's switch to the <@gd.key>Minerals</> tab.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_5: The Refining Unit can <@gd.key>refine</> any material that can be smelted down. Let's try having the Refining Unit process the <@gd.key>Originium Ore</>.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_6: Drag the Originium Ore from the Depot to the <@gd.key>Input Cache</> of the Refining Unit.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_7: The previously idle Refining Unit is now in production and will process raw materials into products over time. Finished products will be placed in the Output Cache to await pickup.
guide_text_open_furnance_storage_8: Once enough has been produced, you can move the products from the Output Cache to your Bag or Depot.
guide_text_open_grinder_1: Click the <@gd.key>Formulas</> button of the Shredding Unit. This special feature makes it easier to operate Endfield Industries tech.
guide_text_open_grinder_2: This shows all the production formulas currently available for this facility.
guide_text_open_grinder_3: We can pin the specific formulas to the main screen.
guide_text_open_grinder_6: The formula is now displayed on the main screen, making it easier to check.
guide_text_open_grinder_7: Keep producing <@gd.key>Origocrust Powder</> and repair the Assembly Station.
guide_text_open_hub_1: The <@gd.key>Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC)</> is the heart and mind of every AIC factory. The PAC screen provides a summary of various AIC operations data.
guide_text_open_hub_2: An operational Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) will continue to generate electricity. <@gd.key>Extra electricity</> will be stored and this shows our current reserves.
guide_text_open_hub_3: This shows the current <@gd.key>Power Output</> and <@gd.key>Power Consumption</> of the AIC.
guide_text_open_hub_4: Click the details button to enter the Electricity Stats screen.
guide_text_open_hub_5: This displays the <@gd.key>Production Speed</> and <@gd.key>Depot Stockpile</> of every AIC product.
guide_text_open_hub_6: Click the details button to enter the Production Stats screen.
guide_text_open_hub_7: Some data here might interest you. Let's take a look at them.
guide_text_open_hub_8: The Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC), just like other AIC facilities, can be moved when required to help you better set up AIC production.
guide_text_open_mfgsta2_1: <@gd.key>Compact Script I</> can provide more experience. Click <@gd.key>Compact Script I</> to start manufacturing.
guide_text_open_mfgsta2_2: The production line we just set up happens to produce the materials required for manufacturing the <@gd.key>Compact Script I</>.
guide_text_open_mfgsta2_3: The production line will keep producing materials. Once we have enough, we can start manufacturing the <@gd.key>Compact Script I</>.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_1: The <@gd.key>Manufacturing Station</> can manufacture items that provide Operator experience.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_2: The <@gd.key>Intel Cassette I</> is currently selected.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_3: The upper right corner indicates the amount of time required to manufacture an <@gd.key>Intel Cassette I</>.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_4: You can use this to change the amount of Cassettes to manufacture.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_5: Click Start when you have enough materials. The Manufacturing Station will start manufacturing the <@gd.key>Intel Cassettes</>.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_6: You must manually pickup Manufacturing Station products. Keep in mind that the Manufacturing Station has a very limited cache.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_7: Once the specified amount of <@gd.key>Intel Cassettes</> are manufactured or when the cache is full, the Manufacturing Station will end its work.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_8: Wait for Cassette manufacturing to complete. Click [Claim] to get the manufactured Cassettes.
guide_text_open_mfgsta_9: Don't forget to assign Operators to this facility to support Manufacturing Station production.
guide_text_open_miner2_1: <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rigs</> can use belt-free transfer to deliver mined ore straight to the Mossfield Depot. No manual work is required.
guide_text_open_miner2_2: Once deployed, <@gd.key>Electric Mining Rigs</> are set to belt-free transfer mode by default. You can also click to switch to storage mode.
guide_text_open_miner2_3: Once you switch to storage mode, it will behave like an Originium Rig and simply store the mined ore in the cache instead of delivering it to the Depot. You'll have to manually pickup the ore from the cache.
guide_text_open_miner2_4: However, if you want to take advantage of automation, it is best to stick to using the belt-free transfer mode.
guide_text_open_miner_1: The <@gd.key>Originium Rig</> will stop working when its cache is full. Visit the <@gd.key>Originium Rig</> every now and then to pick up the mined Originium Ore.
guide_text_open_miner_2: Different areas will have different types of minerals and mineral densities. When density is below 100%, mining efficiency will be lower than the standard production rate of the Mining Rig.
guide_text_open_miner_3: The Originium Ore mined can be used to produce <@gd.key>Origocrust Powder</> for researching AIC Management Plan tech upgrades. Feel free to try that out.
guide_text_open_power_pole_1: You can click the <@gd.key>[Move]</> button to move the Relay Tower.
guide_text_open_power_pole_2: You can also click the <@gd.key>[Stash]</> button to undeploy the Relay Tower and place its beacon in the Bag. <@gd.key>No loss will be incurred</>. You can deploy the Relay Tower again by using the beacon in the Bag.
guide_text_open_power_pole_3: Moving the Relay Tower removes existing electric transmission connections, so you will have to reconnect the transmission cables again.
guide_text_open_procsta_10: Go to the Endfield Database (EFDB) then click <@gd.key>Materials</> to open the Materials screen. The right side of this screen gives a detailed list of <@gd.key>production formulas</> using the selected material.
guide_text_open_procsta_11: This part shows the <@gd.key>formulas for creating</> the selected material. Use this feature when you need to find specific formulas.
guide_text_open_procsta_12: When you're in the Endfield Database (EFDB), click here to <@gd.key>pin</> the formula on the main screen to make them easier to check.
guide_text_open_procsta_2: Gear Formulas acquired from exploration must be analyzed by the Assembly Station before they can be used for production.
guide_text_open_procsta_4: Click the <@gd.key>Active PPE Suit I</> formula.
guide_text_open_procsta_5: You can see that crafting the <@gd.key>Active PPE Suit I</> requires Origocrust Powder and Krystallite Fiber.
guide_text_open_procsta_6: Click the <@gd.key>Krystallite Fiber</> to see how it's produced.
guide_text_open_procsta_7: We can see that <@gd.key>Krystallite Fiber</> can be obtained by refining <@gd.key>Krystallite</>.
guide_text_open_procsta_8: When you find a new material, refer to their tags to check the production processes they can be a part of. Try that out.
guide_text_open_procsta_9: You can also click the <@gd.key>Endfield Database (EFDB)</> (the search button at the upper left), then click <@gd.key>Materials</> to open the Materials screen to check the details.
guide_text_open_seedcol_1: <@gd.key>Seed-Picking Units</> can pick seeds from non-rare species of flora.
guide_text_open_seedcol_2: Click the <@gd.key>Formulas</> button to check on the species of flora that the Seed-Picking Unit can cultivate and harvest from.
guide_text_open_seedcol_3: This shows all formulas currently available to the Seed-Picking Unit. You can see that the facility produces seeds and plant matter at the same time.
guide_text_open_seedcol_4: Click to close the Formulas window.
guide_text_open_seedcol_5: For the Input Cache, place a non-rare species of plant such as <@gd.key>Buckflower or Citrome</>.
guide_text_open_seedcol_6: The Seed-Picking Unit yields 2 products at a time. The <@gd.key>Upper Output Cache</> yields <@gd.key>seeds</> that can be placed in a Planting Unit or a Plot to cultivate them.
guide_text_open_seedcol_7: The <@gd.key>Bottom Output Cache</> yields <@gd.key>Plant Matter</> that can be sent to the Refining Unit to generate Carbon.
guide_text_open_seedcol_8: Try placing plants in the Input Cache to pick some seeds.
guide_text_open_sp_rec_1: Click to replace item to recycle, then select the recyclable materials to fill up the Recycling Station with.
guide_text_open_sp_rec_2: This shows you a list of recyclable materials. When you have enough of them, you can click [Auto Fill] to dump them in.
guide_text_open_sp_rec_4: If you have filled it with recyclables, wait for the recycling to complete. You can then pickup the recycled resources from this place.
guide_text_open_sp_trd1: To support our growth and that of the entire area, we can trade with the 2 largest factions in this area.
guide_text_open_sp_trd10: Don't forget to assign Operators to this facility to support Trading Station performance.
guide_text_open_sp_trd2: Click here to enter into a contract with a faction.
guide_text_open_sp_trd3: Different factions demand different goods. Let's choose <@gd.key>Endfield Industries Engineering Division (EF-ED)</> first.
guide_text_open_sp_trd4: This shows the range of values of orders resulting from this contract.
guide_text_open_sp_trd5: The EF-ED mainly needs food and medication. The orders we receive from them tend to require items within that range.
guide_text_open_sp_trd5_1: For example, we can take <@gd.key>G1 Recovery Cocktails</> we made along during exploration to treat Operators, or supply them to the EF-ED to satisfy the order.
guide_text_open_sp_trd5_2: This is our payment for completing the order requirements.
guide_text_open_sp_trd6: Different items are worth different order values. Once we supply enough items to meet the total order value required, we'll complete the order.
guide_text_open_sp_trd8: We will receive an order after waiting for a while.
guide_text_open_sp_trd9: If you stash a Trading Station, you will <@gd.key>lose all orders already acquired and must get new orders again</>. You will keep current orders if you only move the Trading Station around.
guide_text_planter_intro_desc_1:
The <@gd.key>Planting Unit</> can plant the seeds of non-rare species of flora and raise them to maturity. 
Typical products: <@gd.key>Buckflower and Citrome</>.
guide_text_player_attack_0: Approach the enemy and click the <@gd.key>left mouse button</> <sprite name=mouse0> to perform basic attack. A string of basic attacks form a basic attack sequence.
guide_text_player_attack_1: Approach the enemy and click the <@gd.key>left mouse button</> <sprite name=mouse0> to perform basic attack. A string of basic attacks form a basic attack sequence.
guide_text_player_attack_2: Approach the enemy to automatically switch the target.
guide_text_player_skill: Use number key <sprite name=keypad1> to cast skills. Move the mouse cursor to choose the direction or target and click the <@gd.key>left mouse button</><sprite name=mouse0> to cast the skill at the chosen direction or target.
guide_text_player_skillS: Once SP is maxed, use number key <sprite name=keypad1> to cast the <@gd.key>ultimate</>.
guide_text_player_skillS_1: Successfully hitting an enemy with a skill gives <@gd.key>1 Skill Point (SP)</>. Once SP is maxed, the <@gd.key>ultimate skill</> becomes available for casting.
guide_text_player_skill_n: Use number key <sprite name=keypad1> to cast a skill.
guide_text_player_skill_n2: After skill casting, immediately perform a basic attack <sprite name=mouse0> to unleash a pursuit attack.
guide_text_player_skill_n3: Cast your skills and perform pursuit attacks wisely to quickly defeat your enemies.
guide_text_player_skilladd: After successfully casting a skill, the next basic attack <sprite name=mouse0> performed will be a powerful pursuit attack.
guide_text_player_skilltarget: Cast the skill at the target direction.
guide_text_power_gate_intro_desc_1: Once the <@gd.key>Sector Relay Tower</> is connected to the electrical grid and receives power, it can then relay electricity to <@gd.key>another Sector Relay Tower in another area</>. This will expand the electrical grid of the Automated Industry Complex (AIC) to that area.
guide_text_power_link_1: Now for the final step! We now only need to connect the Relay Coil to the <@gd.key>Relay Tower to power it</>.
guide_text_power_link_des_1: Operators can start pulling the cable of an <@gd.key>Relay Tower connected to the electrical grid</> and connect the cable to the port of another Relay Tower to bring it into the electrical grid as well.
guide_text_power_link_des_2: You cannot transmit electricity from a Relay Tower not connected to the electrical grid.
guide_text_power_sta_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Thermal Bank</> can burn materials with the [Energy] tag to generate electricity for the electrical grid.
guide_text_power_sta_intro_desc_2: Electricity generated by the <@gd.key>Thermal Bank</> must be rectified by the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) before it could be used by the electrical grid. Hence, the <@gd.key>Thermal Bank</> must be connected to the electrical grid in order to start working.
guide_text_ppole_place_1: Use hotkey <sprite name=keypad1> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the <@gd.key>Relay Tower</> in the hotbar to deploy the facility.
guide_text_ppole_place_2: Refer to the guide and move the <@gd.key>Relay Tower</> to the <@gd.key>blinking Relay Tower hologram</> in the environment.
guide_text_ppole_place_3: Click <sprite name=mouse0> [Confirm] to place the <@gd.key>Relay Tower</> in the environment.
guide_text_ppole_qkbar_1: Endministrator, construction is the Endfield Industries' strength made manifest.
guide_text_ppole_qkbar_2: Use hotkey <sprite name=g> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the icon to switch to <@gd.key>AIC Mode</>.
guide_text_ppole_qkbar_3: Use hotkey <sprite name=b> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the icon to open the bag.
guide_text_ppole_qkbar_4: Notice that the bottom hotbar has changed. In <@gd.key>AIC Mode</>, you will get a hotbar specialized for various facilities.
guide_text_ppole_qkbar_5: Refer to the guide and drag the <@gd.key>Relay Tower</> into the hotbar.
guide_text_ppole_qkbar_6: Click here to close the bag and start constructing.
guide_text_qkbar_elcarea_intro_des_1: You can drag facilities from the Bag or Depot into the hotbar. Then you can just click the facility in the hotbar to construct it. <@gd.key>You will have different hotbars inside and outside the Core AIC Area as the facilities available are also different.</>
guide_text_qkbar_elcarea_intro_des_2: Use hotkey <sprite name=q> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> to open the Facility List and select the facility you wish to construct.
guide_text_qkbar_elcarea_intro_des_3: Most facilities must be connected to the electrical grid in order to work. Hence, when constructing facilities, any part of the facility must be within the <@gd.key>Electric Pylon connection area</> to connect the facility to the electrical grid. The <@gd.key>Electric Pylon</> itself must also be connected to the electrical grid via other <@gd.key>Electric Pylons</>, the <@gd.key>Relay Tower</>, or the <@gd.key>Protocol Anchor-Core</>.
guide_text_qkbar_elcarea_intro_title_3: Facilities and connecting them to the electrical grid
guide_text_repair_des_1: You may encounter broken facilities that must be <@gd.key>repaired</>.
guide_text_repair_des_2: Use the Endministrator's special powers and spend certain materials to <@gd.key>repair</> them. Repaired facilities can then be used normally.
guide_text_scan_1: Click <sprite name=mouse2> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the scan icon to scan the surroundings.
guide_text_scan_3: When exploring, scan the area to find interactive objects near you.
guide_text_scan_4: Common interactive objects will be marked blue while quest items will be marked yellow.
guide_text_seedcol_intro2_desc_1: The Seed-Picking Unit yields 2 different products, so its outputs will be slightly different. <@fac.blue>The 3 blue Outputs on the left yield plant seeds</>, while the <@fac.green>3 green Outputs on the right yield Plant Matter</>.
guide_text_seedcol_intro2_desc_2:
The left and right sides of the Seed-Picking Unit can be connected to separate transport belts to send the products to other facilities. 
For example, seeds can be sent to the Planting Unit to be raised as crops, while <@gd.key>Plant Matter can be sent to the Refining Unit</>.
guide_text_seedcol_intro_desc_1:
The <@gd.key>Seed-Picking Unit</> can harvest seeds from non-rare species of flora and provide seeds. 
Typical products: <@gd.key>Buckflower Seed</> as well as <@gd.key>Plant Matter</>.
guide_text_shaper_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Moulding Unit</> can process materials with the [Mould] tag, making products often used in meds or food production. Typical product: <@gd.key>Krystallite Bottle</>.
guide_text_show_cando_1: Feel free to make your preparations before talking to Director Qin Jiangchi.
guide_text_show_cando_des_1: Explore and collect <@gd.key>Ætherium</> and <@gd.key>Æther Manifest</> scattered throughout the world and defeat powerful enemies to earn rewards.
guide_text_show_cando_des_2: Complete the <@gd.key>AIC Management Plan</> to improve your Operator's performance.
guide_text_show_cando_des_3: Explore the environment and find ways to unlock new areas.
guide_text_show_electric_out_1: The AIC is now completely out of electricity!
guide_text_show_electric_out_2:
Deploy <@gd.key>Thermal Banks</> quickly and connect them to the electrical grid to improve Power Output. 
You can also shut down certain facilities to lower Power Consumption temporarily.
guide_text_show_loader_1:
Use the Bus Loader to load materials into the Logistics Bus. 
Items loaded into the Bus will be immediately delivered to the Depot.
guide_text_show_unloader_1:
Use the Bus Unloader to unload materials from the Logistics Bus into the Core AIC Area. 
The Bus Unloader lets you retrieve materials from the Depot from any point of the Logistics Bus.
guide_text_sneakattack_1: If an enemy is <@gd.key>not alerted</>, your attack will stun them, giving you a starting advantage for the battle.
guide_text_sneakattack_3: If an enemy is not alerted, your attack will stun them, giving you a starting advantage for the battle.
guide_text_sp_mfg_intro_desc_1:
The <@gd.key>Manufacturing Station</> can manufacture products that cannot be instantly made using Crafting. Simply provide the materials and wait for a while for the manufacturing process to finish. 
Valuable materials will be separately stashed so they must be <@gd.key>picked up as soon as possible</>.
guide_text_sp_mfg_intro_desc_2: Assign Operators to improve production efficiency or output.
guide_text_sp_rec_intro_desc_1:
We acquired this unique facility from a certain ... strange individual. It is capable of bi-directional material transfer. 
You might find recyclable items while exploring. These items can be recycled at the <@gd.key>Recycling Station</>.
guide_text_sp_rec_intro_desc_2:
Once the items sent through the <@gd.key>Recycling Station</> reaches a specific total value, you will receive 1 valuable reward. 
However, these rewards are pretty random.
guide_text_sp_rec_intro_desc_3: Recycling Stations will also offer to buy pieces of gear from you. Keep in mind that the prices will not be very competitive.
guide_text_sp_trd_commit_order1: This shows the total value requirement of the order. Select enough items with a total value that meets this requirement to satisfy the order.
guide_text_sp_trd_commit_order2: You can also click [Auto Fill] to fill this order automatically.
guide_text_sp_trd_commit_order3: Once you meet the order's total value requirement, you can submit the order and receive payment for that order.
guide_text_sp_trd_intro_desc_1: Endfield can trade with other factions via the <@gd.key>Trading Station</>. Each faction tends to have certain types of products they prefer.
guide_text_sp_trd_intro_desc_2: Every order comes with a value and desired product(s). Simply provide amounts of the desired product(s) that meet the value to submit the order.
guide_text_sp_trd_intro_desc_3:
You can assign Operators to improve Trading Station efficiency or total orders. The Trading Station can also be upgraded to expand order categories. 
REMINDER: Quality orders typically desire expensive products.
guide_text_splitter_intro_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Splitter</> can evenly split item flow from 1 transport belt to 2 or 3 (max) transport belts.
guide_text_splitter_intro_desc_2: You can go to the Facility List to select the <@gd.key>Splitter</>. You can either place it directly or drag it into the hotbar.
guide_text_start_bomb2: Get close to a barrel of explosive materials to make some explosives. Then move the camera to aim and press the attack button to throw.
guide_text_start_bomb3: Explosives can <@gd.key>blow up debris blocking roads</>. They can also deal damage and stun enemies.
guide_text_start_sneak_2: The spectral signal device has made the Aggeloi a lot more dangerous. <@gd.key>Avoid its alert zones</>, make use of cover, and proceed carefully.
guide_text_start_techtree_0: The <@gd.key>AIC Management Plan</> is part of the AIC. It is only visible after using hotkey <sprite name=g> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> clicking the AIC Mode icon.
guide_text_start_techtree_1: Use hotkey <sprite name=t> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the <@gd.key>AIC Management Plan</> tech tree icon (at the upper left).
guide_text_start_techtree_2: The <@gd.key>AIC Management Plan</> offers a detailed overview of AIC development plans. Every node from top to bottom will give your AIC improved performance and options.
guide_text_start_techtree_3: The <@gd.key>AIC Management Plan</> is divided into 3 phases. Every node in a phase must be activated before you can progress to the next phase.
guide_text_start_techtree_4: Let's preview the target tech. <@gd.key>Smart MFG I</> will unlock the <@gd.key>Manufacturing Station</> that can manufacture EXP items for our Operators.
guide_text_start_techtree_5: <@gd.key>3D Assembly II</> unlocks a better Assembly Station that can produce better gear.
guide_text_start_techtree_6: There are many tech nodes. Feel free to come back here to go through them at your own leisure.
guide_text_start_techtree_7: The AIC Management Plan tech nodes can only be unlocked in the specified order. Let's go back to <@gd.key>Basic FAC</> and start from this very first node.
guide_text_start_techtree_8: Once we complete researching the first tech, we can construct <@gd.key>Electric Pylons</>, but we need to deploy 2 <@gd.key>Originium Rigs</> to support our research.
guide_text_storage_base_1: We can move items from our Bag to the <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</> for temporary storage. Items in the Stash will remain there until you retrieve them.
guide_text_storage_base_des_1: Place any items from the Bag in the <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</>. If the Stash is connected to the electrical grid, it will have a more advanced feature...
guide_text_storage_hint_1: Your Bag now has a <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</> beacon that you just received. Open the Bag, then click the beacon to use it.
guide_text_storage_mission_desc1: Drag items out of the <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</> to retrieve them. You can also place items in the <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</>.
guide_text_storage_mission_txt1: The medication is placed in the <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</>. It awaits your retrieval.
guide_text_storager_intro2_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Protocol Stash</> lets you store things from the Bag. If connected to the electrical grid, it will charge over time. When fully charged, you can use <@gd.key>Belt-free Transfer</> to send Protocol Stash contents to the Depot, making it useful in explorations.
guide_text_team_1: A new Operator has joined us. Add her to the team.
guide_text_team_10: Feel free to try things out.
guide_text_team_2: Use hotkey <sprite name=esc> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here to open the <@gd.key>Endfield Multi-Purpose Field Terminal</>.
guide_text_team_3: Go to the Team screen. You can also use hotkey <sprite name=u> to open the screen directly in most circumstances.
guide_text_team_5: Select the Operator who just joined us.
guide_text_team_7: Use the <@gd.key>Team</> feature to view and change your team of field Operators. Deploying the right team of Operators will help you overcome different challenges in the world.
guide_text_team_8: Click the <@gd.key>Quick Team</> to edit the entire team as well.
guide_text_team_9: As you explore, you will receive more Operators who can join your exploration efforts.
guide_text_teamfight_2:
When fighting as a team, your team mates will automatically attack the enemies. 
Using a team mate's skill will automatically swap that team mate in as the Operator you control. 
Now join Perlica and defeat the enemies together.
guide_text_tech_all_finish_1:
Congratulations, Endministrator. You have researched every technology of the current <@gd.key>AIC Management Plan</>! 
Thank you for your hard work!
guide_text_tech_sp_rec_1: We have acquired additional technology beyond the Endfield Industries AIC Management Plan. Let's take a look.
guide_text_tech_sp_rec_3: Every Special Tech we acquire will be displayed here.
guide_text_tempshop_focus_1: Endmin, click here to visit the <@gd.key>Technical Test Exchange</> to recruit new Operators or trade for new weapons. This feature is only available in this Technical Test.
guide_text_thickener_intro_desc_1:
The <@gd.key>Grinding Unit</> can process materials with the [Grind] tag and produces finely-grained powders. 
Typical output: <@gd.key>Cryston Powder</> and <@gd.key>Ground Buckflower Powder</>.
guide_text_tools_asm_mc_intro_desc_1:
The <@gd.key>Packaging Unit</> can process materials with the [Package] tag and produces explosive devices or batteries. 
Typical output: <@gd.key>LC Battery</>.
guide_text_travelpole_intro2_desc_1: The <@gd.key>Zipline Tower</> is an improved version of the Zipline Pylon. It sports a larger radius and allows you to zip through multiple <@gd.key>Zipline Towers</> in a single trip.
guide_text_travelpole_intro2_desc_2: After latching onto a <@gd.key>Zipline Tower</>, you can select a <@gd.key>Zipline Tower</> within zipping range as the next "node", and so on. You can only select a <@gd.key>Zipline Tower</> as the "node" once. Multiple "nodes" can also be selected to create a one-way zipline.
guide_text_travelpole_intro2_desc_3: Once you start zipping down the zipline, when you approach a <@gd.key>Zipline Tower</>, <@gd.key>button queue(s)</> will appear on screen. Press the button within the time limit to choose the direction you want and easily <@gd.key>continue zipping without stopping</>.
guide_text_travelpole_intro_desc_1: Place a <@gd.key>Zipline Pylon</> within the maximum radius of another and make sure nothing is blocking the path between them. You can now rapidly zip your way between these Zipline Pylons.
guide_text_travelpole_intro_desc_2: <@gd.key>Zipline Pylons</> are quite expensive to maintain, so you can only place a limited number of them in a single area. Plan your ziplines carefully.
guide_text_usb_loader_unloader_intro_des: The AIC Logistics Bus uses the <@gd.key>Bus Loader</> and <@gd.key>Bus Unloader</> to move items into and out of the Depot respectively.
At the <@gd.key>Bus Unloader</> select the item you wish to take out of the Depot via the AIC Logistics Bus for processing in the AIC.
The <@gd.key>Bus Loader</> and <@gd.key>Bus Unloader</> can be freely placed so long as they are right next to the AIC Logistics Bus.
guide_text_use_item_1: There are 2 ways to use treatment items.
guide_text_use_item_2: Use hotkey <sprite name=b> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the icon to open the bag.
guide_text_use_item_3: Click here to select the <@gd.key>Buckflower Powder</>.
guide_text_use_item_4: Items can be used in the Bag window. The hotbar also offers a quicker way to use items.
guide_text_use_item_5: Refer to the guide and drag the consumable item into the hotbar.
guide_text_use_item_6: Click here to close the Bag.
guide_text_use_item_7: Use hotkey <sprite name=e> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click the hotbar item to use it without opening the Bag.
guide_text_valuable_equip_1: Pieces of gear are stored in the Valuables Stash. Use hotkey <sprite name=v> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here to open it.
guide_text_valuable_weapon_1: Weapons, weapon essences, and gear will be stored at the [Valuables Stash]. Use hotkey <sprite name=v> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> to open it.
guide_text_watch_1: Let's check the map that Director Qin Jiangchi marked for us. Use hotkey <sprite name=esc> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here to open the <@gd.key>Protocol Synchronizer</>(Protosync).
guide_text_watch_2: The Protosync also has other features that you will eventually use as you explore. Let's click on the Map in the middle of the Protosync first.
guide_text_watch_3: This is the region of <@gd.key>Mossfield</> that we are currently in, though we have yet to acquire map data on certain areas. Let's check the locations that Director Qin Jiangchi marked for us. Click the <@gd.key>Hub Base</>.
guide_text_watch_4: Director Qin mentioned that Andre is in the <@gd.key>Warehouse</> area, which should be nearby.
guide_text_watch_5: But we must go here first to <@gd.key>bring electricity back online</>. You can click the mission point to check the current mission details.
guide_text_watch_6: Feel free to click here and return to the map to check on the <@gd.key>Mossfield</> region. But right now, we should first review the situation in the <@gd.key>Hub Base</>.
guide_text_weapon_1: We acquired a new weapon. Battles will get harder so having better weapons will be necessary. Use hotkey <sprite name=c> or <sprite name=alt>+<sprite name=mouse0> click here.
guide_text_weapon_2: Click on the Weapon screen to see the details of this weapon.
guide_text_weapon_3: This shows the various properties and features of this weapon.
guide_text_weapon_4: Select Swap Weapon to equip the new weapon we acquired.
guide_text_weapon_5: You can select the new weapon to equip here. Most would tend to select weapons that are rarer or features better properties.
guide_text_weapon_6: The Enhance option lets us <@gd.key>Upgrade</> and <@gd.key>Activate</> weapons to make them stronger.
guide_text_weapon_7: The <@gd.key>Upgrade</> option lets you consume weapon progression materials OR other weapons to level up a weapon.
guide_text_weapon_7_1: Keep in mind an activated weapon cannot be used as a leveling material for other weapons. To use it for leveling, you must first unequip its Essence.
guide_text_weapon_8: The <@gd.key>Activate feature lets you apply an activating material known as the Essence upon the weapon.
guide_text_weapon_9: After battles, you might find activating materials from the defeated enemies. These materials can be applied on a weapon and enhance its properties.
guide_text_weapon_pic_01: <@gd.key>Upgrade</> weapons by consuming weapon progression materials or other weapons you don't need. Upgraded or attuned weapons will have significantly improved properties.
guide_text_weapon_pic_02: If you upgrade a weapon with another upgraded weapon, all experience materials and T-Creds consumed for the latter will be returned to you.
guide_text_weapon_pic_03: Defeated enemies may sometimes drop activating materials known as the <@gd.key>Essence</>. Apply the <@gd.key>Essence</> on a weapon to significantly boost weapon properties or even weapon skills.
guide_text_winder_intro_des_1:
The <@gd.key>Inlaying Unit</> can process materials with the [Inlay] tag, making products often used in various electric parts. 
Typical output: <@gd.key>Inlaid Krystallite Component</>.
guide_text_xmode_desc_1:
In AIC Mode, use the hotkey <sprite name=x> to switch to the <@gd.key>Dismantling Mode</>. 
In Dismantling Mode, you can use the hotkey <sprite name=f> to quickly dismantle and stash facilities selected in an area. All facility contents will also be <@gd.key>placed in your Depot</>, Endministrator.
guide_title_bigCannon_01: Careful. Certain enemies may <@gd.key>bombard</> a target on a <@gd.key>regular basis</>.
guide_title_bigCannon_02: Keep moving and don't drag out the battle.
guide_title_energy_01: Spatial distortions will generate <@gd.key>plenty of Elemental Orbs</> but enemies also have greatly increased ATK.
guide_title_energy_02: <@gd.key>Triggering Elemental Orbs</> and setting off elemental fusion effects will be key.
guide_title_glassCannon_01: Enemies have <@gd.key>greatly reduced</> Stability but they also have greatly increased ATK.
guide_title_glassCannon_02: Consider <@gd.key>breaking enemy Stability</> quickly and applying <@gd.key>crowd control</> statuses as frequently as you can.
guide_title_noUseitem_01: Careful. You <@gd.key>cannot use any medication</> in this Rift.
guide_title_noUseitem_02: Defeating enemies in <@gd.key>each area</> will <@gd.key>recover your HP</>.
guide_title_open_statistic_3: Filtering and setting time for stats displayed
guide_title_poison_01: Watch out for the <@gd.key>toxic mist</>. Operators caught within it will <@gd.key>continue to lose HP</>.
guide_title_poison_02: <@gd.key>Each enemy defeat</> can <@gd.key>recover</> a certain amount of HP.
lang_int_005_chips_toast: Omega chip connected. Activate scan to read data.
lang_int_rubbishking_scanned: [Target item detected in vicinity. Preliminary analysis: Goodies from Norbert!]

mission_mai_e1m10_description: The Endfielders finally recovered the missing backup parts for the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC). The Endministrator's team must activate this facility to activate the Automated Industry Complex (AIC), the crown jewel of Endfield Industries. However, the AIC is often referred to as the "factory".
mission_mai_e1m10_q#30_obj_0_description: Look for Andre outside the Base.
mission_mai_e1m1_description: The road is burning. Enemies known as the Aggeloi are destroying everything they can find. The Endministrator and Perlica must reach the Hub Base quickly... Hopefully they'll be there in time to salvage the situation.
mission_mai_e1m1_q#3_obj_0_description: Get to the Hub Base quickly.
mission_mai_e1m1_q#4_obj_0_description: Get to the Hub Base quickly.
mission_mai_e1m1_q#5_obj_0_description: Get to the Hub Base quickly.
mission_mai_e1m1_q#6_obj_0_description: Get to the Hub Base quickly.
mission_mai_e1m1_q#7_obj_0_description: Get to the Hub Base quickly.
mission_mai_e1m1_q#8_obj_0_description: Defeat the Aggeloi in the Base.
mission_mai_e1m2_description: To restore order to Base Command, the Endministrator must defeat enemies within the Base and quickly learn how certain facilities work.
mission_mai_e1m3_description: The Endministrator and Perlica are now heading to the Hub Base Warehouse to rescue the trapped tech expert named Andre. Enemies in the Warehouse are extremely dangerous. Discretion is advised.
mission_mai_e1m4_description: Andre is now safe and Chen just joined up. To repair the Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) and look for the Rhodes Island Operator, the Endministrator's team must head to the northwest of the Base.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#0_obj_0_description: Look for Andre by the Hub entrance.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#10_obj_0_description: Talk to the machinist at the Endfield mining site.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#11_obj_0_description: Attack <@qu.key>Originium formations</> and gather 5 parcels of Originium Ore.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#11_obj_1_description: Attack <@qu.key>trees</> and gather 5 parcels of Mossfield Wood.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#12_obj_0_description: Craft a pair of basic explorer gloves.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#1_obj_0_description: Talk to Andre to learn about the situation.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#2_obj_0_description: Go to the clearing and check on the PAC.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#3-2_obj_0_description: Talk to Wulfgard and confirm the possible Landbreaker hideouts.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#4_obj_0_description: Go to the first Landbreaker hideout to find the supplies.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#5_obj_0_description: Go to the next Landbreaker hideout to find the supplies.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#6_obj_0_description: Defeat the Landbreakers and retrieve the Endfield supplies.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#7_obj_0_description: Keep looking for the looted Endfield supplies.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#8-1_obj_0_description: Look for evidence near the nomads.
mission_mai_e1m4_q#8_obj_0_description: Use the Protosync to scan for evidence and clues.
mission_mai_e1m5_description: The frontier is home to all sorts of people, so don't be judgmental when dealing with them. The Endministrator's team is about to meet up with another group of nomads who are very different from the ones they first met.
mission_mai_e1m5_q#10-2_obj_0_descriptio: Use the floating rocks to jump across the pit and reach the other side.
mission_mai_e1m5_q#10_obj_0_description: Find another route to get through.
mission_mai_e1m5_q#11_obj_0_description: Approach the Blight Core and destroy it.
mission_mai_e1m5_q#2_obj_0_description: Follow Soth to the temporary nomad camp.
mission_mai_e1m5_q#4_obj_0_description: Open the Bag, then deploy the Protocol Stash in any empty area.

mission_mai_e2m6_description: After acquiring basic intel from Perlica, the Endministrator's team entered an old, abandoned UWST mine and readied themselves to destroy the Ankhors. Nobody expected to see new Originium growths in this area, as the Old Mines were supposed to have dried up...
mission_mai_e2m7_description: After hearing Chen's scouting report, the Endministrator's team entered the severely Blighted Chasm to destroy the Ankhors. The Chasm Area used to be an intersection of multiple highways. Violent changes to the terrain, active Blight, and Ankhor pulses have now made this place extremely hazardous.
mission_mai_e2m8_description: Every finding discovered leads to this Originium Gorge. It feels as though everything happened on purpose to lead the Endministrator to this place. Press onward, and enter the Gorge.
mission_mai_e2m9_description: One of the 3 Ankhors is destroyed. The Endministrator's team arrived at an intersection. The road straight ahead is blocked by a mysterious energy barrier. Destroying the Ankhor seems to weaken this barrier... Anyway, just choose one of the 2 paths available to you.
mission_sid_e1m1_description: The Endministrator met a logistics machinist in the Hub Base Logistics Transfer area who seems to have a bit of a problem on his hands...

mission_sid_e2m5_description: The UWST bred an experimental creature known as the Terrell in the areas outside the meteorological station of the Originium Research Park. This creature is larger than the typical langohrs. Follow it as it seems to have a gift for finding rare plants.

scene_map01_dg005_tips_1: Suspended crates in the area have been reset
scene_map01_lv001_sub02_tips_1: Found some cargo left on the carriage, but it's stuck at a place inaccessible by climbing.
scene_map01_lv001_sub02_tips_2: Try contacting the logistics staff and describe the situation.
scene_map01_lv002_dexasco_skill: The <@gd.key>red halo</> indicates that Cliff is charging his skill. Use <@gd.key>crowd control skills (like those that inflict Knock Down or Lifted)</> to interrupt him.
scene_map01_lv005_sub01_tips_1: Access Card required to activate this.
tips_bat_1: Some powerful enemies will have a Mantle effect when they cast skills. This would prevent you from inflicting crowd control effects upon them. However, this does not apply when red circles appear.
tips_bat_10: Any Elemental Orb can be triggered by any area-of-effect (AOE) elemental skill to trigger different elemental fusion effects.
tips_bat_11: When certain Operators (the Endministrator, for now) attain certain Breakthroughs, they will unlock an additional main skill. You can switch between these main skills in the Overview screen.
tips_bat_2: When a red circle appears around an enemy, your crowd control skills (those that inflict Lift, Knock Down, or Knock Back) will interrupt enemy skill casting, even if the enemy has an active Mantle effect.
tips_bat_3: The main skills and ultimates of certain Operators are channeling skills. Switching to another Operator during the casting of channeling skills does not interrupt the casting.
tips_bat_4: Explosives thrown by Roving Shooters can trigger elemental fusions as if it is a Thermal Orb.
tips_bat_5: An Operator will be forced to exit the battle when their HP is zeroed. You can quickly use this Operator's skill and consume a Resuscitant in your Bag.
tips_bat_6: You can use the middle mouse button to move the camera during the time stop for skill casting.
tips_bat_7: Press the skill button twice to cast your skill on the default target.
tips_bat_8: A knocked back enemy that collides with an obstacle suffers additional Physical Damage and Break.
tips_bat_9: After skill casting, the Operator can gain an empowered pursuit attack. However, this effect is removed if you switch to another Operator before performing this empowered attack.
tips_doo_1: If you need certain materials for construction or progression, try exploring and find materials to gather. You might just get lucky.
tips_doo_2: Seeds and similar materials you gather can be planted. Test Plots are the only way that we can grow rare species of flora.
tips_doo_3: Character breakthrough and skill upgrade materials can only be acquired by gathering or planting.
tips_doo_4: Certain items you gather can be processed at Crafting or the AIC into various products to help you explore and fight.
tips_doo_5: Certain plants and materials in the world will grow back as time passes, allowing you to gather them again.
tips_doo_6: There are many useless pieces of "junk" in the world, but someone is always willing to recycle them at the Recycling Station.
tips_fac_1: Many world objects are gatherable and even destructible. Never hurts to swing your arms around sometimes, Endministrator.
tips_fac_2: If AIC Power Consumption exceeds its Power Output, the AIC will consume the electrical grid reserves. Once all reserves are depleted, all buildings will be forced to shut down.
tips_fac_3: Make clever use of transport belts and belt-free transfer to automate your AIC.
tips_fac_4: The AIC produces all sorts of things. Improving your AIC tech will enhance your Operators' performance and make exploration easier than before.
tips_fac_5: To have electricity outside the Protocol Testing Base, we must transmit power to the Sector Relay Tower of the target area.
tips_fac_6: Every Operator has their AIC skill. Assign them to the right facility to improve its production efficiency or lower its consumption.
tips_nar_1: Endfield Industries (or Endfield for short) is an industrial company that specializes in terraforming inhospitable regions and building new settlements.
tips_nar_10: Be part of the Area Stability Project to help those who are in need.
tips_nar_11: Many in the frontier wilderness seek resources and artifacts buried underground.
tips_nar_13: Various kinds of monstrosities often appear in severely Blighted areas.
tips_nar_14: The Blight is an anomalous phenomenon unique to Talos-II.
tips_nar_15: Originium is an energy-rich crystalline material commonly found throughout Talos-II.
tips_nar_2: The Automated Industry Complex (AIC) developed by Endfield Industries has made survival possible for many people.
tips_nar_3: Hongshan Science Academy is an organization of intellectuals who focus on scientific exploration and research. They search for a solution to address various natural and ecological issues of the planet.
tips_nar_4: Most Landbreakers are dangerous, but some of them might entertain the idea of negotiation.
tips_nar_5: In Talos-II, the cost of Oripathy treatment has been greatly reduced.
tips_nar_6: Better bring a decent weapon if you intend to explore the wilderness on your own.
tips_nar_7: Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a company that focuses on medical and pharmaceutical research.
tips_nar_8: Armed Messengers play a role in guaranteeing safe and secure delivery of various goods in Talos-II.
tips_nar_9: The Order of Steel Oath is a militarized monastic organization fighting to protect humanity against the monstrous, the mutants, and the Blighted ones.

ui_fac_proc_new_hint:
Artifice Points will only be spent upon successful assembly of an Artifice Gear. The more artificing work you perform on a piece of gear, the better your chances in assembling a piece of Artifice Gear. Successful crafting of Artifice Gear resets this to initial levels. 
You gain Artifice Points with time.
ui_fac_proc_old_hint: Artifice Mode gives you a chance to assemble Artifice Gear. Successful artificing spends Artifice Points.

ui_nar_ending_toast_main: The main story of this Arknights: Endfield Technical Test has ended! Thanks for sharing this journey with us, Endministrator! You can still explore the map and discover new things. Hope to see you again in Talos-II.

ui_set_gamesetting_show_output: Show/Hide product icons on facilities (in AIC Mode)
ui_set_gamesetting_tech_tree: Open AIC Management Plan (in AIC Mode)
ui_set_gamesetting_top_view: Switch to top view (in AIC Mode)
ui_talent_break_enhance: Promote to Rank 3 to boost effects
ui_wiki_building_hub1: The only source of electricity in the Core AIC Area. The PAC transmits power to other Relay Towers and Electric Pylons, thereby supplying electricity to other facilities.
ui_wiki_building_hub2: Click on [Facility details] to view the Electrical Grid Report, Production Report, and achievement data. These screens will tell you the consumption and generation of electricity of the AIC as well as production of various products.
ui_wiki_building_hub3: Finish researching the [Belt Basics] tech to unlock the Output and Input ports.
ui_wiki_building_hub4: Output Ports: The Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) has a total of 6 Output Ports that can unload a selected material/product from the Depot. These can then be transported to another facility via transport belts.
ui_wiki_building_hub5: Inputs: The Protocol Anchor-Core (PAC) has a total of 14 Inputs. Transport belts can be used to transport materials/products from facilities to the PAC Input so that they can be stored in the Depot.
ui_wiki_building_proc1: Uses material to assemble gear. At higher levels, the Assembly Station can craft better quality gear.
ui_wiki_building_proc2: Reach Assembly Station level 3 to unlock Fuse and Recast options for Essences.
ui_wiki_building_proc3: Reach Assembly Station level 4 to unlock Artifice Mode when assembling high quality gear.
